  2014	   *
  2015	   * @return array
  2016	   *   Array of canonical URLs.
  2017	   */
  2018	  protected function getCanonicalUrls(): array {
  2019	    if (function_exists('ilas_site_assistant_get_canonical_urls')) {
  2020	      return ilas_site_assistant_get_canonical_urls();
  2021	    }
  2022	    return [
  2023	      'apply' => '/apply-for-help',
  2024	      'hotline' => '/Legal-Advice-Line',
  2025	      'offices' => '/contact-us',
  2026	      'feedback' => '/get-involved/feedback',
  2027	      'forms' => '/forms',
  2028	      'guides' => '/guides',
  2029	      'faq' => '/faq',
  2030	      'resources' => '/what-we-do/resources',
  2031	      'services' => '/services',
  2032	      'donate' => '/donate',
  2033	      'senior_risk_detector' => '/senior-risk-detector',
  2034	      'service_areas' => [
  2035	        'housing' => '/legal-help/housing',
  2036	        'family' => '/legal-help/family',
  2037	        'seniors' => '/legal-help/seniors',
  2038	        'health' => '/legal-help/health',
  2039	        'consumer' => '/legal-help/consumer',
  2040	        'civil_rights' => '/legal-help/civil-rights',
  2041	      ],
  2042	    ];
  2043	  }
  2044	
  2045	}

===== web/modules/custom/ilas_site_assistant/src/Service/TopicRouter.php =====
     1	<?php
     2	
     3	namespace Drupal\ilas_site_assistant\Service;
     4	
     5	/**
     6	 * Routes short/single-token queries to canonical topic landing pages.
     7	 *
     8	 * This service runs BEFORE generic fallback in the IntentRouter pipeline.
     9	 * It handles the case where users type a bare topic word like "divorce",
    10	 * "custody", or "eviction" which would otherwise fall through to
    11	 * greeting/fallback because existing phrase detection requires multi-word
    12	 * triggers.
    13	 *
    14	 * Matching hierarchy (highest to lowest confidence):
    15	 *   1. Exact token match (0.88)
    16	 *   2. Stem match (0.85)
    17	 *   3. Synonym match (0.82)
    18	 *   4. Short phrase match (0.85)
    19	 *   5. Fuzzy Levenshtein match (0.70) - very conservative thresholds
    20	 *
    21	 * This class has NO Drupal service dependencies beyond cache, so it can be
    22	 * used standalone in eval harnesses and unit tests.
    23	 */
    24	class TopicRouter {
    25	
    26	  /**
    27	   * Confidence scores by match type.
    28	   */
    29	  const CONFIDENCE_EXACT = 0.88;
    30	  const CONFIDENCE_STEM = 0.85;
    31	  const CONFIDENCE_PHRASE = 0.85;
    32	  const CONFIDENCE_SYNONYM = 0.82;
    33	  const CONFIDENCE_FUZZY = 0.70;
    34	
    35	  /**
    36	   * Maximum word count for messages this router handles.
    37	   *
    38	   * Beyond this, the message is complex enough for IntentRouter patterns.
    39	   */
    40	  const MAX_WORD_COUNT = 3;
    41	
    42	  /**
    43	   * Minimum token length for fuzzy matching.
    44	   */
    45	  const MIN_FUZZY_LENGTH = 5;
    46	
    47	  /**
    48	   * The loaded topic map.
    49	   *
    50	   * @var array
    51	   */
    52	  protected $topicMap;
    53	
    54	  /**
    55	   * Precomputed lookup indices.
    56	   *
    57	   * @var array
    58	   */
    59	  protected $tokenIndex;
    60	  protected $stemIndex;
    61	  protected $synonymIndex;
    62	  protected $phraseIndex;
    63	  protected $fuzzyTokens;
    64	
    65	  /**
    66	   * Topic-level alias index mapping aliases to canonical keys.
    67	   *
    68	   * @var array
    69	   */
    70	  protected $topicAliasIndex;
    71	
    72	  /**
    73	   * Cache backend.
    74	   *
    75	   * @var \Drupal\Core\Cache\CacheBackendInterface|null
    76	   */
    77	  protected $cache;
    78	
    79	  /**
    80	   * Constructs a TopicRouter.
    81	   *
    82	   * @param object|null $cache
    83	   *   Optional cache backend. NULL for standalone/test usage.
    84	   */
    85	  public function __construct($cache = NULL) {
    86	    $this->cache = $cache;
    87	    $this->loadTopicMap();
    88	    $this->buildIndices();
    89	  }
    90	
    91	  /**
    92	   * Routes a short message to a topic if possible.
    93	   *
    94	   * @param string $message
    95	   *   The user's message (raw input).
    96	   *
    97	   * @return array|null
    98	   *   Topic routing result with keys:
    99	   *     - type: 'topic_routed'
   100	   *     - topic: Topic key (e.g., 'family', 'housing')
   101	   *     - service_area: Service area key
   102	   *     - url: Canonical landing page URL
   103	   *     - label: Human-readable label
   104	   *     - confidence: Match confidence (0-1)
   105	   *     - match_type: How the match was made (exact|stem|synonym|phrase|fuzzy)
   106	   *     - matched_token: The token that matched
   107	   *   Or NULL if no topic match.
   108	   */
   109	  public function route(string $message): ?array {
   110	    $normalized = $this->normalize($message);
   111	
   112	    // Guard: reject messages with too many raw words (before stop word
   113	    // removal). TopicRouter is designed for short, 1-3 word queries.
   114	    // A longer sentence belongs in IntentRouter even if most words are
   115	    // stop words.
   116	    $raw_words = preg_split('/\s+/', $normalized, -1, PREG_SPLIT_NO_EMPTY);
   117	    if (count($raw_words) > self::MAX_WORD_COUNT) {
   118	      return NULL;
   119	    }
   120	
   121	    $words = $this->tokenize($normalized);
   122	    $word_count = count($words);
   123	
   124	    // No meaningful tokens after stop word removal.
   125	    if ($word_count === 0) {
   126	      return NULL;
   127	    }
   128	
   129	    // For single-token queries, try all match types.
   130	    if ($word_count === 1) {
   131	      $token = $words[0];
   132	      return $this->matchSingleToken($token);
   133	    }
   134	
   135	    // For 2-3 word queries, try phrase match first, then per-token.
   136	    $phrase_result = $this->matchPhrase($normalized);
   137	    if ($phrase_result) {
   138	      return $phrase_result;
   139	    }
   140	
   141	    // Try matching each token individually, return best match.
   142	    $best = NULL;
   143	    foreach ($words as $word) {
   144	      // Skip very short words (likely stop words).
   145	      if (strlen($word) < 3) {
   146	        continue;
   147	      }
   148	      $result = $this->matchSingleToken($word);
   149	      if ($result && (!$best || $result['confidence'] > $best['confidence'])) {
   150	        $best = $result;
   151	      }
   152	    }
   153	
   154	    // For multi-word queries, slightly reduce confidence since context is ambiguous.
   155	    if ($best) {
   156	      $best['confidence'] = max(0.65, $best['confidence'] - 0.05);
   157	    }
   158	
   159	    return $best;
   160	  }
   161	
   162	  /**
   163	   * Matches a single token against all indices.
   164	   *
   165	   * @param string $token
   166	   *   Normalized single token.
   167	   *
   168	   * @return array|null
   169	   *   Match result or NULL.
   170	   */
   171	  protected function matchSingleToken(string $token): ?array {
   172	    // 1. Exact token match.
   173	    if (isset($this->tokenIndex[$token])) {
   174	      $topic_key = $this->tokenIndex[$token];
   175	      return $this->buildResult($topic_key, self::CONFIDENCE_EXACT, 'exact', $token);
   176	    }
   177	
   178	    // 2. Stem match.
   179	    $stem_result = $this->matchStem($token);
   180	    if ($stem_result) {
   181	      return $stem_result;
   182	    }
   183	
   184	    // 3. Synonym match.
   185	    if (isset($this->synonymIndex[$token])) {
   186	      $topic_key = $this->synonymIndex[$token];
   187	      return $this->buildResult($topic_key, self::CONFIDENCE_SYNONYM, 'synonym', $token);
   188	    }
   189	
   190	    // 4. Fuzzy match (very conservative).
   191	    $fuzzy_result = $this->matchFuzzy($token);
   192	    if ($fuzzy_result) {
   193	      return $fuzzy_result;
   194	    }
   195	
   196	    return NULL;
   197	  }
   198	
   199	  /**
   200	   * Matches a token against stem patterns.
   201	   *
   202	   * @param string $token
   203	   *   Normalized token.
   204	   *
   205	   * @return array|null
   206	   *   Match result or NULL.
   207	   */
   208	  protected function matchStem(string $token): ?array {
   209	    foreach ($this->stemIndex as $stem => $topic_key) {
   210	      if (strlen($token) > strlen($stem) && strpos($token, $stem) === 0) {
   211	        return $this->buildResult($topic_key, self::CONFIDENCE_STEM, 'stem', $token);
   212	      }
   213	    }
   214	    return NULL;
   215	  }
   216	
   217	  /**
   218	   * Matches a normalized phrase against phrase patterns.
   219	   *
   220	   * @param string $normalized
   221	   *   Normalized message.
   222	   *
   223	   * @return array|null
   224	   *   Match result or NULL.
   225	   */
   226	  protected function matchPhrase(string $normalized): ?array {
   227	    foreach ($this->phraseIndex as $phrase => $topic_key) {
   228	      if (strpos($normalized, $phrase) !== FALSE) {
   229	        return $this->buildResult($topic_key, self::CONFIDENCE_PHRASE, 'phrase', $phrase);
   230	      }
   231	    }
   232	    return NULL;
   233	  }
   234	
   235	  /**
   236	   * Fuzzy matches a token using Levenshtein distance.
   237	   *
   238	   * Very conservative: max distance 1 for 5-7 char tokens, 2 for 8+ chars.
   239	   * Tokens under 5 characters are not fuzzy-matched to avoid false positives.
   240	   *
   241	   * @param string $token
   242	   *   Normalized token.
   243	   *
   244	   * @return array|null
   245	   *   Match result or NULL.
   246	   */
   247	  protected function matchFuzzy(string $token): ?array {
   248	    $len = strlen($token);
   249	    if ($len < self::MIN_FUZZY_LENGTH) {
   250	      return NULL;
   251	    }
   252	
   253	    $max_distance = $len >= 8 ? 2 : 1;
   254	    $best_distance = $max_distance + 1;
   255	    $best_topic = NULL;
   256	    $best_match = NULL;
   257	
   258	    foreach ($this->fuzzyTokens as $candidate => $topic_key) {
   259	      // Only fuzzy-match against tokens of similar length.
   260	      $candidate_len = strlen($candidate);
   261	      if (abs($len - $candidate_len) > $max_distance) {
   262	        continue;
   263	      }
   264	
   265	      $distance = levenshtein($token, $candidate);
   266	      if ($distance > 0 && $distance <= $max_distance && $distance < $best_distance) {
   267	        $best_distance = $distance;
   268	        $best_topic = $topic_key;
   269	        $best_match = $candidate;
   270	      }
   271	    }
   272	
   273	    if ($best_topic !== NULL) {
   274	      // Scale confidence down by distance.
   275	      $confidence = self::CONFIDENCE_FUZZY - ($best_distance - 1) * 0.05;
   276	      return $this->buildResult($best_topic, $confidence, 'fuzzy', $best_match);
   277	    }
   278	
   279	    return NULL;
   280	  }
   281	
   282	  /**
   283	   * Builds a routing result array.
   284	   *
   285	   * @param string $topic_key
   286	   *   The topic key (e.g., 'family', 'housing').
   287	   * @param float $confidence
   288	   *   Match confidence.
   289	   * @param string $match_type
   290	   *   How the match was made.
   291	   * @param string $matched_token
   292	   *   The token/phrase that matched.
   293	   *
   294	   * @return array
   295	   *   Routing result.
   296	   */
   297	  protected function buildResult(string $topic_key, float $confidence, string $match_type, string $matched_token): array {
   298	    $topic = $this->topicMap[$topic_key];
   299	    return [
   300	      'type' => 'topic_routed',
   301	      'topic' => $topic_key,
   302	      'service_area' => $topic['service_area'],
   303	      'url' => $topic['url'],
   304	      'label' => $topic['label'],
   305	      'confidence' => $confidence,
   306	      'match_type' => $match_type,
   307	      'matched_token' => $matched_token,
   308	    ];
   309	  }
   310	
   311	  /**
   312	   * Normalizes a message for matching.
   313	   *
   314	   * @param string $message
   315	   *   Raw message.
   316	   *
   317	   * @return string
   318	   *   Normalized lowercase string with punctuation removed.
   319	   */
   320	  protected function normalize(string $message): string {
   321	    $message = strtolower(trim($message));
   322	    // Remove punctuation except hyphens (for compound words).
   323	    $message = preg_replace('/[^\w\s\-]/u', '', $message);
   324	    // Normalize whitespace.
   325	    $message = preg_replace('/\s+/', ' ', $message);
   326	    return $message;
   327	  }
   328	
   329	  /**
   330	   * Tokenizes a normalized message into words.
   331	   *
   332	   * @param string $normalized
   333	   *   Normalized message.
   334	   *
   335	   * @return array
   336	   *   Array of tokens, with stop words removed.
   337	   */
   338	  protected function tokenize(string $normalized): array {
   339	    $words = preg_split('/\s+/', $normalized, -1, PREG_SPLIT_NO_EMPTY);
   340	
   341	    // Remove stop words for matching (but keep for phrase matching).
   342	    $stop_words = [
   343	      'a', 'an', 'the', 'and', 'or', 'of', 'to', 'in', 'for', 'on', 'with',
   344	      'is', 'are', 'i', 'you', 'we', 'they', 'my', 'your', 'me', 'can',
   345	      'do', 'does', 'did', 'have', 'has', 'am', 'be', 'been', 'what',
   346	      'how', 'where', 'when', 'why', 'this', 'that', 'it', 'about',
   347	      'need', 'help', 'want', 'looking', 'get', 'find',
   348	      'por', 'el', 'la', 'los', 'las', 'un', 'una', 'con', 'de', 'en',
   349	    ];
   350	
   351	    return array_values(array_filter($words, function ($word) use ($stop_words) {
   352	      return !in_array($word, $stop_words);
   353	    }));
   354	  }
   355	
   356	  /**
   357	   * Loads the topic map from YAML config.
   358	   */
   359	  protected function loadTopicMap(): void {
   360	    // Check cache first.
   361	    if ($this->cache) {
   362	      $cached = $this->cache->get('ilas_topic_router_map');
   363	      if ($cached) {
   364	        $this->topicMap = $cached->data;
   365	        return;
   366	      }
   367	    }
   368	
   369	    $yaml_path = __DIR__ . '/../../config/routing/topic_map.yml';
   370	    if (!file_exists($yaml_path)) {
   371	      $this->topicMap = [];
   372	      return;
   373	    }
   374	
   375	    $yaml_content = file_get_contents($yaml_path);
   376	    // Use Symfony YAML parser if available, otherwise basic parsing.
   377	    if (class_exists('\Symfony\Component\Yaml\Yaml')) {
   378	      $this->topicMap = \Symfony\Component\Yaml\Yaml::parse($yaml_content) ?: [];
   379	    }
   380	    else {
   381	      // Fallback: use Drupal's Yaml parser.
   382	      if (class_exists('\Drupal\Component\Serialization\Yaml')) {
   383	        $this->topicMap = \Drupal\Component\Serialization\Yaml::decode($yaml_content) ?: [];
   384	      }
   385	      else {
   386	        $this->topicMap = [];
   387	      }
   388	    }
   389	
   390	    // Cache the parsed map.
   391	    if ($this->cache && !empty($this->topicMap)) {
   392	      $this->cache->set('ilas_topic_router_map', $this->topicMap, time() + 3600);
   393	    }
   394	  }
   395	
   396	  /**
   397	   * Builds precomputed lookup indices from the topic map.
   398	   */
   399	  protected function buildIndices(): void {
   400	    $this->tokenIndex = [];
   401	    $this->stemIndex = [];
   402	    $this->synonymIndex = [];
   403	    $this->phraseIndex = [];
   404	    $this->fuzzyTokens = [];
   405	    $this->topicAliasIndex = [];
   406	
   407	    foreach ($this->topicMap as $topic_key => $topic) {
   408	      // Index exact tokens.
   409	      foreach ($topic['tokens'] ?? [] as $token) {
   410	        $normalized = strtolower($token);
   411	        $this->tokenIndex[$normalized] = $topic_key;
   412	        // Also add to fuzzy candidates.
   413	        $this->fuzzyTokens[$normalized] = $topic_key;
   414	      }
   415	
   416	      // Index stems.
   417	      foreach ($topic['stems'] ?? [] as $stem) {
   418	        $this->stemIndex[strtolower($stem)] = $topic_key;
   419	      }
   420	
   421	      // Index synonyms.
   422	      foreach ($topic['synonyms'] ?? [] as $synonym) {
   423	        $normalized = strtolower($synonym);
   424	        $this->synonymIndex[$normalized] = $topic_key;
   425	        // Also add to fuzzy candidates.
   426	        $this->fuzzyTokens[$normalized] = $topic_key;
   427	      }
   428	
   429	      // Index phrases.
   430	      foreach ($topic['phrases'] ?? [] as $phrase) {
   431	        $this->phraseIndex[strtolower($phrase)] = $topic_key;
   432	      }
   433	
   434	      // Index topic-level aliases for canonical key resolution.
   435	      foreach ($topic['topics'] ?? [] as $sub_key => $sub_topic) {
   436	        $entry = [
   437	          'service_area' => $topic_key,
   438	          'topic' => $sub_key,
   439	          'canonical_key' => $sub_topic['canonical_key'],
   440	          'term_uuid' => $sub_topic['term_uuid'] ?? NULL,
   441	          'term_name' => $sub_topic['term_name'] ?? NULL,
   442	        ];
   443	        // Index the sub-key itself (e.g., "divorce", "eviction").
   444	        $this->topicAliasIndex[strtolower($sub_key)] = $entry;
   445	        // Index the term_name.
   446	        if (!empty($sub_topic['term_name'])) {
   447	          $this->topicAliasIndex[strtolower($sub_topic['term_name'])] = $entry;
   448	        }
   449	        // Index each alias.
   450	        foreach ($sub_topic['aliases'] ?? [] as $alias) {
   451	          $this->topicAliasIndex[strtolower($alias)] = $entry;
   452	        }
   453	      }
   454	    }
   455	  }
   456	
   457	  /**
   458	   * Returns the loaded topic map (for debugging/testing).
   459	   *
   460	   * @return array
   461	   *   The topic map.
   462	   */
   463	  public function getTopicMap(): array {
   464	    return $this->topicMap;
   465	  }
   466	
   467	  /**
   468	   * Returns all registered topics with their URLs.
   469	   *
   470	   * @return array
   471	   *   Associative array of topic_key => ['url' => ..., 'label' => ...].
   472	   */
   473	  public function getTopicUrls(): array {
   474	    $urls = [];
   475	    foreach ($this->topicMap as $key => $topic) {
   476	      $urls[$key] = [
   477	        'url' => $topic['url'],
   478	        'label' => $topic['label'],
   479	      ];
   480	    }
   481	    return $urls;
   482	  }
   483	
   484	  /**
   485	   * Resolves a message to a canonical topic key.
   486	   *
   487	   * Looks up the normalized message (and its individual tokens) against
   488	   * the topic-level alias index built from topic_map.yml `topics:` entries.
   489	   *
   490	   * @param string $message
   491	   *   The user's message (raw input).
   492	   *
   493	   * @return array|null
   494	   *   Topic resolution result with keys:
   495	   *     - service_area: Service area key (e.g., 'family')
   496	   *     - topic: Topic sub-key (e.g., 'divorce')
   497	   *     - canonical_key: Stable identifier (e.g., 'family_divorce')
   498	   *     - term_uuid: UUID of the topics taxonomy term (or NULL)
   499	   *     - term_name: Exact Drupal term name (or NULL)
   500	   *   Or NULL if no topic-level match.
   501	   */
   502	  public function resolveTopicKey(string $message): ?array {
   503	    $normalized = $this->normalize($message);
   504	
   505	    // 1. Try full normalized message as a phrase match.
   506	    if (isset($this->topicAliasIndex[$normalized])) {
   507	      return $this->topicAliasIndex[$normalized];
   508	    }
   509	
   510	    // 2. Try individual tokens (after stop word removal).
   511	    $words = $this->tokenize($normalized);
   512	    foreach ($words as $word) {
   513	      if (isset($this->topicAliasIndex[$word])) {
   514	        return $this->topicAliasIndex[$word];
   515	      }
   516	    }
   517	
   518	    return NULL;
   519	  }
   520	
   521	  /**
   522	   * Returns all canonical topic keys with their metadata.
   523	   *
   524	   * @return array
   525	   *   Flat array of canonical key entries, each with:
   526	   *     - canonical_key, service_area, topic, term_uuid, term_name.
   527	   */
   528	  public function getAllCanonicalKeys(): array {
   529	    $keys = [];
   530	    foreach ($this->topicMap as $topic_key => $topic) {
   531	      foreach ($topic['topics'] ?? [] as $sub_key => $sub_topic) {
   532	        $keys[] = [
   533	          'service_area' => $topic_key,
   534	          'topic' => $sub_key,
   535	          'canonical_key' => $sub_topic['canonical_key'],
   536	          'term_uuid' => $sub_topic['term_uuid'] ?? NULL,
   537	          'term_name' => $sub_topic['term_name'] ?? NULL,
   538	        ];
   539	      }
   540	    }
   541	    return $keys;
   542	  }
   543	
   544	  /**
   545	   * Returns the topic alias index (for debugging/testing).
   546	   *
   547	   * @return array
   548	   *   The topic alias index.
   549	   */
   550	  public function getTopicAliasIndex(): array {
   551	    return $this->topicAliasIndex;
   552	  }
   553	
   554	}

===== web/modules/custom/ilas_site_assistant/src/Service/FallbackGate.php =====
     1	<?php
     2	
     3	namespace Drupal\ilas_site_assistant\Service;
     4	
     5	use Drupal\Core\Config\ConfigFactoryInterface;
     6	
     7	/**
     8	 * Service for determining when to fallback to LLM (Gemini).
     9	 *
    10	 * This service implements a measurable, tunable confidence model that combines:
    11	 * - Intent confidence (rule-based pattern match strength)
    12	 * - Retrieval confidence (FAQ/resource search result quality)
    13	 * - Safety flags (urgent situations that override normal routing)
    14	 *
    15	 * The gate decides one of four outcomes:
    16	 * 1. ANSWER - Built-in response with high confidence
    17	 * 2. CLARIFY - Ask for clarification before proceeding
    18	 * 3. FALLBACK_LLM - Route to Gemini for help
    19	 * 4. HARD_ROUTE - Safety/policy override to specific resource
    20	 *
    21	 * Reason codes explain WHY a decision was made for debugging and tuning.
    22	 */
    23	class FallbackGate {
    24	
    25	  /**
    26	   * Decision types.
    27	   */
    28	  const DECISION_ANSWER = 'answer';
    29	  const DECISION_CLARIFY = 'clarify';
    30	  const DECISION_FALLBACK_LLM = 'fallback_llm';
    31	  const DECISION_HARD_ROUTE = 'hard_route';
    32	
    33	  /**
    34	   * Reason codes - explain why a decision was made.
    35	   */
    36	  const REASON_HIGH_CONF_INTENT = 'HIGH_CONF_INTENT';
    37	  const REASON_HIGH_CONF_RETRIEVAL = 'HIGH_CONF_RETRIEVAL';
    38	  const REASON_LOW_INTENT_CONF = 'LOW_INTENT_CONF';
    39	  const REASON_LOW_RETRIEVAL_SCORE = 'LOW_RETRIEVAL_SCORE';
    40	  const REASON_AMBIGUOUS_MULTI_INTENT = 'AMBIGUOUS_MULTI_INTENT';
    41	  const REASON_SAFETY_URGENT = 'SAFETY_URGENT';
    42	  const REASON_OUT_OF_SCOPE = 'OUT_OF_SCOPE';
    43	  const REASON_POLICY_VIOLATION = 'POLICY_VIOLATION';
    44	  const REASON_NO_RESULTS = 'NO_RESULTS';
    45	  const REASON_LARGE_SCORE_GAP = 'LARGE_SCORE_GAP';
    46	  const REASON_BORDERLINE_CONF = 'BORDERLINE_CONF';
    47	  const REASON_GREETING = 'GREETING';
    48	  const REASON_LLM_DISABLED = 'LLM_DISABLED';
    49	
    50	  /**
    51	   * The config factory.
    52	   *
    53	   * @var \Drupal\Core\Config\ConfigFactoryInterface
    54	   */
    55	  protected $configFactory;
    56	
    57	  /**
    58	   * Default thresholds.
    59	   *
    60	   * These can be overridden in config.
    61	   *
    62	   * @var array
    63	   */
    64	  protected $defaultThresholds = [
    65	    // Intent confidence thresholds.
    66	    'intent_high_conf' => 0.85,
    67	    'intent_medium_conf' => 0.65,
    68	    'intent_low_conf' => 0.40,
    69	
    70	    // Retrieval score thresholds.
    71	    'retrieval_high_score' => 0.75,
    72	    'retrieval_medium_score' => 0.50,
    73	    'retrieval_low_score' => 0.30,
    74	
    75	    // Score gap thresholds (difference between top result and others).
    76	    'retrieval_score_gap_high' => 0.25,
    77	    'retrieval_score_gap_low' => 0.10,
    78	
    79	    // Minimum results to consider retrieval confident.
    80	    'retrieval_min_results' => 1,
    81	
    82	    // Combined confidence thresholds.
    83	    'combined_high_conf' => 0.80,
    84	    'combined_fallback_threshold' => 0.50,
    85	
    86	    // Message characteristics.
    87	    'short_message_words' => 3,
    88	    'ambiguous_message_words' => 2,
    89	  ];
    90	
    91	  /**
    92	   * Safety flags that trigger hard routing.
    93	   *
    94	   * @var array
    95	   */
    96	  protected $urgentSafetyFlags = [
    97	    'dv_indicator',
    98	    'eviction_imminent',
    99	    'crisis_emergency',
   100	    'identity_theft',
   101	    'deadline_pressure',
   102	  ];
   103	
   104	  /**
   105	   * Intent types that don't need retrieval to confirm.
   106	   *
   107	   * @var array
   108	   */
   109	  protected $directRouteIntents = [
   110	    'greeting',
   111	    'apply',
   112	    'apply_for_help',
   113	    'hotline',
   114	    'legal_advice_line',
   115	    'offices',
   116	    'offices_contact',
   117	    'donate',
   118	    'donations',
   119	    'feedback',
   120	    'services',
   121	    'services_overview',
   122	    'eligibility',
   123	    'risk_detector',
   124	    'high_risk',
   125	    'out_of_scope',
   126	    'clarify',
   127	    'disambiguation',
   128	    'forms_inventory',
   129	    'guides_inventory',
   130	    'services_inventory',
   131	    'service_area',
   132	    'navigation',
   133	  ];
   134	
   135	  /**
   136	   * Intent types that benefit from retrieval confirmation.
   137	   *
   138	   * @var array
   139	   */
   140	  protected $retrievalIntents = [
   141	    'faq',
   142	    'forms',
   143	    'forms_finder',
   144	    'guides',
   145	    'guides_finder',
   146	    'resources',
   147	    'topic',
   148	    'service_area',
   149	    'topic_employment',
   150	    'topic_housing',
   151	    'topic_family',
   152	    'topic_seniors',
   153	    'topic_benefits',
   154	    'topic_health',
   155	    'topic_consumer',
   156	    'topic_civil_rights',
   157	    // Sub-topic intents benefit from retrieval confirmation.
   158	    'topic_family_custody',
   159	    'topic_family_divorce',
   160	    'topic_family_child_support',
   161	    'topic_family_protection_order',
   162	    'topic_housing_eviction',
   163	    'topic_housing_foreclosure',
   164	    'topic_consumer_debt_collection',
   165	    'topic_consumer_bankruptcy',
   166	  ];
   167	
   168	  /**
   169	   * Constructs a FallbackGate object.
   170	   *
   171	   * @param \Drupal\Core\Config\ConfigFactoryInterface $config_factory
   172	   *   The config factory.
   173	   */
   174	  public function __construct(ConfigFactoryInterface $config_factory) {
   175	    $this->configFactory = $config_factory;
   176	  }
   177	
   178	  /**
   179	   * Evaluates whether to answer, clarify, or fallback to LLM.
   180	   *
   181	   * @param array $intent
   182	   *   The detected intent from IntentRouter.
   183	   * @param array $retrieval_results
   184	   *   Search results from FAQ/resource search (with scores).
   185	   * @param array $safety_flags
   186	   *   Safety flags detected from the message.
   187	   * @param array $context
   188	   *   Additional context (message, policy check, etc.).
   189	   *
   190	   * @return array
   191	   *   Decision array with keys:
   192	   *   - 'decision': One of DECISION_* constants
   193	   *   - 'reason_code': One of REASON_* constants
   194	   *   - 'confidence': Overall confidence score (0-1)
   195	   *   - 'details': Debug details about the decision
   196	   */
   197	  public function evaluate(array $intent, array $retrieval_results, array $safety_flags, array $context = []): array {
   198	    $thresholds = $this->getThresholds();
   199	    $message = $context['message'] ?? '';
   200	    $policy_violation = $context['policy_violation'] ?? FALSE;
   201	
   202	    // Initialize decision details for debugging.
   203	    $details = [
   204	      'intent_type' => $intent['type'] ?? 'unknown',
   205	      'intent_confidence' => $this->calculateIntentConfidence($intent, $message),
   206	      'retrieval_confidence' => $this->calculateRetrievalConfidence($retrieval_results, $thresholds),
   207	      'safety_flags' => $safety_flags,
   208	      'thresholds_used' => $thresholds,
   209	    ];
   210	
   211	    // Check 1: Policy violation - hard route to escalation.
   212	    if ($policy_violation) {
   213	      return $this->buildDecision(
   214	        self::DECISION_HARD_ROUTE,
   215	        self::REASON_POLICY_VIOLATION,
   216	        1.0,
   217	        $details
   218	      );
   219	    }
   220	
   221	    // Check 2: Safety flags override - hard route with safety resources.
   222	    $urgent_flags = array_intersect($safety_flags, $this->urgentSafetyFlags);
   223	    if (!empty($urgent_flags)) {
   224	      return $this->buildDecision(
   225	        self::DECISION_HARD_ROUTE,
   226	        self::REASON_SAFETY_URGENT,
   227	        1.0,
   228	        array_merge($details, ['triggered_flags' => $urgent_flags])
   229	      );
   230	    }
   231	
   232	    // Check 3: High-risk intent detected - hard route.
   233	    if (($intent['type'] ?? '') === 'high_risk') {
   234	      return $this->buildDecision(
   235	        self::DECISION_HARD_ROUTE,
   236	        self::REASON_SAFETY_URGENT,
   237	        0.95,
   238	        array_merge($details, ['risk_category' => $intent['risk_category'] ?? 'unknown'])
   239	      );
   240	    }
   241	
   242	    // Check 4: Out-of-scope - hard route with explanation.
   243	    if (($intent['type'] ?? '') === 'out_of_scope') {
   244	      return $this->buildDecision(
   245	        self::DECISION_HARD_ROUTE,
   246	        self::REASON_OUT_OF_SCOPE,
   247	        0.90,
   248	        $details
   249	      );
   250	    }
   251	
   252	    // Check 5: Greeting - always answer.
   253	    if (($intent['type'] ?? '') === 'greeting') {
   254	      return $this->buildDecision(
   255	        self::DECISION_ANSWER,
   256	        self::REASON_GREETING,
   257	        1.0,
   258	        $details
   259	      );
   260	    }
   261	
   262	    // Check 6: Unknown intent - evaluate for fallback.
   263	    if (($intent['type'] ?? '') === 'unknown') {
   264	      return $this->handleUnknownIntent($details, $thresholds, $retrieval_results, $message);
   265	    }
   266	
   267	    // Check 7: Direct route intents (high confidence without retrieval).
   268	    if (in_array($intent['type'], $this->directRouteIntents)) {
   269	      $intent_conf = $details['intent_confidence'];
   270	
   271	      if ($intent_conf >= $thresholds['intent_high_conf']) {
   272	        return $this->buildDecision(
   273	          self::DECISION_ANSWER,
   274	          self::REASON_HIGH_CONF_INTENT,
   275	          $intent_conf,
   276	          $details
   277	        );
   278	      }
   279	
   280	      // Medium confidence - still answer but note it.
   281	      if ($intent_conf >= $thresholds['intent_medium_conf']) {
   282	        return $this->buildDecision(
   283	          self::DECISION_ANSWER,
   284	          self::REASON_HIGH_CONF_INTENT,
   285	          $intent_conf,
   286	          array_merge($details, ['confidence_level' => 'medium'])
   287	        );
   288	      }
   289	
   290	      // Direct route intents should still route at low confidence (e.g. short
   291	      // messages like "office locations" that trigger a word-count penalty).
   292	      // These intents don't depend on retrieval, so routing is safe.
   293	      if ($intent_conf >= $thresholds['intent_low_conf']) {
   294	        return $this->buildDecision(
   295	          self::DECISION_ANSWER,
   296	          self::REASON_HIGH_CONF_INTENT,
   297	          $intent_conf,
   298	          array_merge($details, ['confidence_level' => 'low'])
   299	        );
   300	      }
   301	    }
   302	
   303	    // Check 8: Retrieval-based intents - combine intent + retrieval confidence.
   304	    if (in_array($intent['type'], $this->retrievalIntents) || !empty($retrieval_results)) {
   305	      return $this->handleRetrievalIntent($details, $thresholds, $retrieval_results);
   306	    }
   307	
   308	    // Check 9: Borderline cases - evaluate combined confidence.
   309	    return $this->handleBorderlineCase($details, $thresholds, $retrieval_results, $message);
   310	  }
   311	
   312	  /**
   313	   * Handles unknown intent cases.
   314	   *
   315	   * @param array $details
   316	   *   Decision details.
   317	   * @param array $thresholds
   318	   *   Threshold values.
   319	   * @param array $retrieval_results
   320	   *   Retrieval results.
   321	   * @param string $message
   322	   *   Original message.
   323	   *
   324	   * @return array
   325	   *   Decision array.
   326	   */
   327	  protected function handleUnknownIntent(array $details, array $thresholds, array $retrieval_results, string $message): array {
   328	    $retrieval_conf = $details['retrieval_confidence'];
   329	
   330	    // If we have good retrieval results, answer with them.
   331	    if ($retrieval_conf >= $thresholds['retrieval_high_score'] && !empty($retrieval_results)) {
   332	      return $this->buildDecision(
   333	        self::DECISION_ANSWER,
   334	        self::REASON_HIGH_CONF_RETRIEVAL,
   335	        $retrieval_conf,
   336	        $details
   337	      );
   338	    }
   339	
   340	    // Check if message is too short/ambiguous for clarification.
   341	    $word_count = str_word_count($message);
   342	    if ($word_count <= $thresholds['ambiguous_message_words']) {
   343	      return $this->buildDecision(
   344	        self::DECISION_CLARIFY,
   345	        self::REASON_LOW_INTENT_CONF,
   346	        0.3,
   347	        array_merge($details, ['message_words' => $word_count])
   348	      );
   349	    }
   350	
   351	    // Check if LLM is enabled.
   352	    $config = $this->configFactory->get('ilas_site_assistant.settings');
   353	    if (!$config->get('llm.enabled')) {
   354	      // Fallback to clarification since LLM is disabled.
   355	      return $this->buildDecision(
   356	        self::DECISION_CLARIFY,
   357	        self::REASON_LLM_DISABLED,
   358	        0.2,
   359	        $details
   360	      );
   361	    }
   362	
   363	    // Unknown intent with sufficient message length - try LLM.
   364	    return $this->buildDecision(
   365	      self::DECISION_FALLBACK_LLM,
   366	      self::REASON_LOW_INTENT_CONF,
   367	      0.2,
   368	      $details
   369	    );
   370	  }
   371	
   372	  /**
   373	   * Handles retrieval-based intent cases.
   374	   *
   375	   * @param array $details
   376	   *   Decision details.
   377	   * @param array $thresholds
   378	   *   Threshold values.
   379	   * @param array $retrieval_results
   380	   *   Retrieval results.
   381	   *
   382	   * @return array
   383	   *   Decision array.
   384	   */
   385	  protected function handleRetrievalIntent(array $details, array $thresholds, array $retrieval_results): array {
   386	    $intent_conf = $details['intent_confidence'];
   387	    $retrieval_conf = $details['retrieval_confidence'];
   388	
   389	    // No results found.
   390	    if (empty($retrieval_results)) {
   391	      $config = $this->configFactory->get('ilas_site_assistant.settings');
   392	
   393	      // If high intent confidence, still route to the resource page.
   394	      if ($intent_conf >= $thresholds['intent_high_conf']) {
   395	        return $this->buildDecision(
   396	          self::DECISION_ANSWER,
   397	          self::REASON_NO_RESULTS,
   398	          $intent_conf * 0.7,
   399	          array_merge($details, ['note' => 'No results but high intent confidence'])
   400	        );
   401	      }
   402	
   403	      // Low confidence with no results - try LLM or clarify.
   404	      if ($config->get('llm.enabled')) {
   405	        return $this->buildDecision(
   406	          self::DECISION_FALLBACK_LLM,
   407	          self::REASON_NO_RESULTS,
   408	          0.3,
   409	          $details
   410	        );
   411	      }
   412	
   413	      return $this->buildDecision(
   414	        self::DECISION_CLARIFY,
   415	        self::REASON_NO_RESULTS,
   416	        0.3,
   417	        $details
   418	      );
   419	    }
   420	
   421	    // Calculate combined confidence.
   422	    $combined_conf = $this->calculateCombinedConfidence($intent_conf, $retrieval_conf);
   423	    $details['combined_confidence'] = $combined_conf;
   424	
   425	    // Check for score gap (indicates clear winner).
   426	    $score_gap = $this->calculateScoreGap($retrieval_results);
   427	    $details['retrieval_score_gap'] = $score_gap;
   428	
   429	    // High combined confidence with good score gap.
   430	    if ($combined_conf >= $thresholds['combined_high_conf'] &&
   431	        $score_gap >= $thresholds['retrieval_score_gap_low']) {
   432	      return $this->buildDecision(
   433	        self::DECISION_ANSWER,
   434	        self::REASON_HIGH_CONF_RETRIEVAL,
   435	        $combined_conf,
   436	        $details
   437	      );
   438	    }
   439	
   440	    // Large score gap indicates clear best result.
   441	    if ($score_gap >= $thresholds['retrieval_score_gap_high']) {
   442	      return $this->buildDecision(
   443	        self::DECISION_ANSWER,
   444	        self::REASON_LARGE_SCORE_GAP,
   445	        $combined_conf,
   446	        $details
   447	      );
   448	    }
   449	
   450	    // Medium confidence - still answer but may want LLM enhancement.
   451	    if ($combined_conf >= $thresholds['combined_fallback_threshold']) {
   452	      return $this->buildDecision(
   453	        self::DECISION_ANSWER,
   454	        self::REASON_BORDERLINE_CONF,
   455	        $combined_conf,
   456	        array_merge($details, ['suggest_llm_enhancement' => TRUE])
   457	      );
   458	    }
   459	
   460	    // Low confidence - fallback to LLM.
   461	    $config = $this->configFactory->get('ilas_site_assistant.settings');
   462	    if ($config->get('llm.enabled')) {
   463	      return $this->buildDecision(
   464	        self::DECISION_FALLBACK_LLM,
   465	        self::REASON_LOW_RETRIEVAL_SCORE,
   466	        $combined_conf,
   467	        $details
   468	      );
   469	    }
   470	
   471	    // LLM disabled - clarify instead.
   472	    return $this->buildDecision(
   473	      self::DECISION_CLARIFY,
   474	      self::REASON_LOW_RETRIEVAL_SCORE,
   475	      $combined_conf,
   476	      $details
   477	    );
   478	  }
   479	
   480	  /**
   481	   * Handles borderline/edge cases.
   482	   *
   483	   * @param array $details
   484	   *   Decision details.
   485	   * @param array $thresholds
   486	   *   Threshold values.
   487	   * @param array $retrieval_results
   488	   *   Retrieval results.
   489	   * @param string $message
   490	   *   Original message.
   491	   *
   492	   * @return array
   493	   *   Decision array.
   494	   */
   495	  protected function handleBorderlineCase(array $details, array $thresholds, array $retrieval_results, string $message): array {
   496	    $intent_conf = $details['intent_confidence'];
   497	    $retrieval_conf = $details['retrieval_confidence'];
   498	
   499	    // Check for multi-intent ambiguity.
   500	    $word_count = str_word_count($message);
   501	    $has_conjunctions = preg_match('/\b(and|also|plus|or)\b/i', $message);
   502	
   503	    if ($word_count > 10 && $has_conjunctions && $intent_conf < $thresholds['intent_high_conf']) {
   504	      $config = $this->configFactory->get('ilas_site_assistant.settings');
   505	
   506	      if ($config->get('llm.enabled')) {
   507	        return $this->buildDecision(
   508	          self::DECISION_FALLBACK_LLM,
   509	          self::REASON_AMBIGUOUS_MULTI_INTENT,
   510	          $intent_conf,
   511	          array_merge($details, ['multi_intent_indicators' => TRUE])
   512	        );
   513	      }
   514	
   515	      return $this->buildDecision(
   516	        self::DECISION_CLARIFY,
   517	        self::REASON_AMBIGUOUS_MULTI_INTENT,
   518	        $intent_conf,
   519	        array_merge($details, ['multi_intent_indicators' => TRUE])
   520	      );
   521	    }
   522	
   523	    // Default: answer with current intent confidence.
   524	    return $this->buildDecision(
   525	      self::DECISION_ANSWER,
   526	      self::REASON_HIGH_CONF_INTENT,
   527	      $intent_conf,
   528	      $details
   529	    );
   530	  }
   531	
   532	  /**
   533	   * Calculates intent confidence based on match characteristics.
   534	   *
   535	   * @param array $intent
   536	   *   The detected intent.
   537	   * @param string $message
   538	   *   The original message.
   539	   *
   540	   * @return float
   541	   *   Confidence score between 0 and 1.
   542	   */
   543	  public function calculateIntentConfidence(array $intent, string $message): float {
   544	    $type = $intent['type'] ?? 'unknown';
   545	
   546	    // Unknown intent has very low confidence.
   547	    if ($type === 'unknown') {
   548	      return 0.15;
   549	    }
   550	
   551	    // LLM-classified intents have medium confidence.
   552	    if (isset($intent['source']) && $intent['source'] === 'llm') {
   553	      return 0.55;
   554	    }
   555	
   556	    // Start with base confidence for rule-based matches.
   557	    $base_conf = 0.70;
   558	
   559	    // Adjust based on message characteristics.
   560	    $word_count = str_word_count($message);
   561	
   562	    // Very short messages get lower confidence.
   563	    if ($word_count < 3) {
   564	      $base_conf -= 0.15;
   565	    }
   566	    // Medium length messages get a boost.
   567	    elseif ($word_count >= 3 && $word_count <= 15) {
   568	      $base_conf += 0.15;
   569	    }
   570	    // Very long messages may be complex/multi-intent.
   571	    elseif ($word_count > 20) {
   572	      $base_conf -= 0.05;
   573	    }
   574	
   575	    // Check if extraction found relevant keywords.
   576	    if (!empty($intent['extraction']['keywords'])) {
   577	      $keyword_count = count($intent['extraction']['keywords']);
   578	      if ($keyword_count >= 2) {
   579	        $base_conf += 0.05;
   580	      }
   581	    }
   582	
   583	    // Check for phrase matches (more specific = higher confidence).
   584	    if (!empty($intent['extraction']['phrases_found'])) {
   585	      $base_conf += 0.10;
   586	    }
   587	
   588	    // Check for synonym normalization (indicates good understanding).
   589	    if (!empty($intent['extraction']['synonyms_applied'])) {
   590	      $base_conf += 0.05;
   591	    }
   592	
   593	    // Cap at 1.0.
   594	    return min(1.0, max(0.0, $base_conf));
   595	  }
   596	
   597	  /**
   598	   * Calculates retrieval confidence from search results.
   599	   *
   600	   * @param array $results
   601	   *   Search results with scores.
   602	   * @param array $thresholds
   603	   *   Threshold values.
   604	   *
   605	   * @return float
   606	   *   Confidence score between 0 and 1.
   607	   */
   608	  public function calculateRetrievalConfidence(array $results, array $thresholds): float {
   609	    if (empty($results)) {
   610	      return 0.0;
   611	    }
   612	
   613	    // Get the top score.
   614	    $top_score = $this->getTopScore($results);
   615	
   616	    // If we have explicit scores, use them.
   617	    if ($top_score > 0) {
   618	      // Normalize score to 0-1 range if needed.
   619	      if ($top_score > 1) {
   620	        // Assume Search API scores (often 0-100 or higher).
   621	        $top_score = min(1.0, $top_score / 100);
   622	      }
   623	
   624	      return $top_score;
   625	    }
   626	
   627	    // Without explicit scores, estimate based on result count and position.
   628	    $result_count = count($results);
   629	
   630	    if ($result_count >= 3) {
   631	      return 0.70; // Multiple results suggest good match.
   632	    }
   633	    elseif ($result_count >= 1) {
   634	      return 0.50; // At least one result.
   635	    }
   636	
   637	    return 0.0;
   638	  }
   639	
   640	  /**
   641	   * Calculates combined confidence from intent and retrieval.
   642	   *
   643	   * @param float $intent_conf
   644	   *   Intent confidence.
   645	   * @param float $retrieval_conf
   646	   *   Retrieval confidence.
   647	   *
   648	   * @return float
   649	   *   Combined confidence.
   650	   */
   651	  protected function calculateCombinedConfidence(float $intent_conf, float $retrieval_conf): float {
   652	    // Weighted average favoring intent slightly.
   653	    return ($intent_conf * 0.55) + ($retrieval_conf * 0.45);
   654	  }
   655	
   656	  /**
   657	   * Gets the top score from results.
   658	   *
   659	   * @param array $results
   660	   *   Search results.
   661	   *
   662	   * @return float
   663	   *   Top score.
   664	   */
   665	  protected function getTopScore(array $results): float {
   666	    if (empty($results)) {
   667	      return 0.0;
   668	    }
   669	
   670	    $top = $results[0];
   671	
   672	    // Check various score field names.
   673	    if (isset($top['score'])) {
   674	      return (float) $top['score'];
   675	    }
   676	    if (isset($top['relevance'])) {
   677	      return (float) $top['relevance'];
   678	    }
   679	    if (isset($top['_score'])) {
   680	      return (float) $top['_score'];
   681	    }
   682	
   683	    return 0.0;
   684	  }
   685	
   686	  /**
   687	   * Calculates score gap between top result and second result.
   688	   *
   689	   * Large gap indicates a clear "winner" among results.
   690	   *
   691	   * @param array $results
   692	   *   Search results with scores.
   693	   *
   694	   * @return float
   695	   *   Score gap (0-1).
   696	   */
   697	  protected function calculateScoreGap(array $results): float {
   698	    if (count($results) < 2) {
   699	      // Single result = maximum gap (clear winner).
   700	      return 1.0;
   701	    }
   702	
   703	    $top_score = $this->getTopScore($results);
   704	
   705	    // Get second score.
   706	    $second_score = 0.0;
   707	    if (isset($results[1])) {
   708	      $second = $results[1];
   709	      if (isset($second['score'])) {
   710	        $second_score = (float) $second['score'];
   711	      }
   712	      elseif (isset($second['relevance'])) {
   713	        $second_score = (float) $second['relevance'];
   714	      }
   715	    }
   716	
   717	    // Avoid division by zero.
   718	    if ($top_score == 0) {
   719	      return 0.0;
   720	    }
   721	
   722	    return ($top_score - $second_score) / $top_score;
   723	  }
   724	
   725	  /**
   726	   * Builds a decision array.
   727	   *
   728	   * @param string $decision
   729	   *   Decision type.
   730	   * @param string $reason_code
   731	   *   Reason code.
   732	   * @param float $confidence
   733	   *   Confidence score.
   734	   * @param array $details
   735	   *   Additional details.
   736	   *
   737	   * @return array
   738	   *   Decision array.
   739	   */
   740	  protected function buildDecision(string $decision, string $reason_code, float $confidence, array $details): array {
   741	    return [
   742	      'decision' => $decision,
   743	      'reason_code' => $reason_code,
   744	      'confidence' => round($confidence, 4),
   745	      'details' => $details,
   746	    ];
   747	  }
   748	
   749	  /**
   750	   * Gets threshold values from config with defaults.
   751	   *
   752	   * @return array
   753	   *   Threshold values.
   754	   */
   755	  public function getThresholds(): array {
   756	    $config = $this->configFactory->get('ilas_site_assistant.settings');
   757	    $config_thresholds = $config->get('fallback_gate.thresholds') ?? [];
   758	
   759	    return array_merge($this->defaultThresholds, $config_thresholds);
   760	  }
   761	
   762	  /**
   763	   * Gets all reason codes with descriptions.
   764	   *
   765	   * @return array
   766	   *   Reason codes mapped to descriptions.
   767	   */
   768	  public static function getReasonCodeDescriptions(): array {
   769	    return [
   770	      self::REASON_HIGH_CONF_INTENT => 'High confidence intent match from rule-based patterns',
   771	      self::REASON_HIGH_CONF_RETRIEVAL => 'High quality retrieval results support the answer',
   772	      self::REASON_LOW_INTENT_CONF => 'Intent detection confidence too low for reliable answer',
   773	      self::REASON_LOW_RETRIEVAL_SCORE => 'Retrieval results not confident enough',
   774	      self::REASON_AMBIGUOUS_MULTI_INTENT => 'Message appears to contain multiple intents',
   775	      self::REASON_SAFETY_URGENT => 'Safety flags detected requiring urgent routing',
   776	      self::REASON_OUT_OF_SCOPE => 'Request is outside scope of ILAS services',
   777	      self::REASON_POLICY_VIOLATION => 'Message triggered policy filter',
   778	      self::REASON_NO_RESULTS => 'No retrieval results found',
   779	      self::REASON_LARGE_SCORE_GAP => 'Large score gap between top results indicates clear match',
   780	      self::REASON_BORDERLINE_CONF => 'Borderline confidence - answer may benefit from enhancement',
   781	      self::REASON_GREETING => 'Simple greeting detected',
   782	      self::REASON_LLM_DISABLED => 'LLM fallback unavailable - using clarification',
   783	    ];
   784	  }
   785	
   786	  /**
   787	   * Gets metrics about gate decisions for a batch of evaluations.
   788	   *
   789	   * @param array $decisions
   790	   *   Array of decision arrays.
   791	   *
   792	   * @return array
   793	   *   Metrics summary.
   794	   */
   795	  public static function calculateGateMetrics(array $decisions): array {
   796	    $total = count($decisions);
   797	    if ($total === 0) {
   798	      return [
   799	        'total' => 0,
   800	        'answer_rate' => 0,
   801	        'clarify_rate' => 0,
   802	        'fallback_rate' => 0,
   803	        'hard_route_rate' => 0,
   804	        'avg_confidence' => 0,
   805	        'by_reason_code' => [],
   806	      ];
   807	    }
   808	
   809	    $counts = [
   810	      self::DECISION_ANSWER => 0,
   811	      self::DECISION_CLARIFY => 0,
   812	      self::DECISION_FALLBACK_LLM => 0,
   813	      self::DECISION_HARD_ROUTE => 0,
   814	    ];
   815	
   816	    $by_reason = [];
   817	    $conf_sum = 0;
   818	
   819	    foreach ($decisions as $decision) {
   820	      $type = $decision['decision'] ?? '';
   821	      if (isset($counts[$type])) {
   822	        $counts[$type]++;
   823	      }
   824	
   825	      $reason = $decision['reason_code'] ?? 'UNKNOWN';
   826	      $by_reason[$reason] = ($by_reason[$reason] ?? 0) + 1;
   827	
   828	      $conf_sum += $decision['confidence'] ?? 0;
   829	    }
   830	
   831	    // Count dead ends: decisions where no actionable links would be present.
   832	    // NO_RESULTS with low confidence and no LLM fallback = potential dead end.
   833	    $dead_end_count = 0;
   834	    foreach ($decisions as $decision) {
   835	      $reason = $decision['reason_code'] ?? '';
   836	      $conf = $decision['confidence'] ?? 0;
   837	      // A dead end is a low-confidence fallback with no results.
   838	      if ($reason === self::REASON_NO_RESULTS && $conf < 0.4) {
   839	        $dead_end_count++;
   840	      }
   841	    }
   842	
   843	    return [
   844	      'total' => $total,
   845	      'answer_rate' => round($counts[self::DECISION_ANSWER] / $total, 4),
   846	      'clarify_rate' => round($counts[self::DECISION_CLARIFY] / $total, 4),
   847	      'fallback_rate' => round($counts[self::DECISION_FALLBACK_LLM] / $total, 4),
   848	      'hard_route_rate' => round($counts[self::DECISION_HARD_ROUTE] / $total, 4),
   849	      'dead_end_rate' => round($dead_end_count / $total, 4),
   850	      'avg_confidence' => round($conf_sum / $total, 4),
   851	      'by_reason_code' => $by_reason,
   852	    ];
   853	  }
   854	
   855	}

===== web/modules/custom/ilas_site_assistant/src/Service/FallbackTreeEvaluator.php =====
     1	<?php
     2	
     3	namespace Drupal\ilas_site_assistant\Service;
     4	
     5	/**
     6	 * 4-level no-dead-end fallback tree evaluator.
     7	 *
     8	 * Pure-PHP static utility with no Drupal dependencies. Given the current
     9	 * intent, retrieval results, conversation history, and optional TopIntentsPack,
    10	 * determines the appropriate fallback level and returns an actionable response
    11	 * structure with at least 2 links.
    12	 *
    13	 * Levels:
    14	 *   1: Known intent, no results, first attempt → clarifier chips + contact
    15	 *   2: Repeated same-area failure → parent service area page + sub-topic chips
    16	 *   3: 2+ failures → prominent contact info + nearby intents
    17	 *   4: Terminal → direct human connection + all contact channels
    18	 *
    19	 * Invariant: every level includes >= 2 actionable links.
    20	 */
    21	class FallbackTreeEvaluator {
    22	
    23	  /**
    24	   * Default canonical URLs used when none are provided.
    25	   */
    26	  const DEFAULT_URLS = [
    27	    'apply' => '/apply-for-help',
    28	    'hotline' => '/Legal-Advice-Line',
    29	    'offices' => '/contact/offices',
    30	    'services' => '/services',
    31	    'forms' => '/forms',
    32	    'faq' => '/faq',
    33	  ];
    34	
    35	  /**
    36	   * Service area URL map.
    37	   */
    38	  const SERVICE_AREA_URLS = [
    39	    'housing' => '/legal-help/housing',
    40	    'family' => '/legal-help/family',
    41	    'seniors' => '/legal-help/seniors',
    42	    'health' => '/legal-help/health',
    43	    'consumer' => '/legal-help/consumer',
    44	    'civil_rights' => '/legal-help/civil-rights',
    45	  ];
    46	
    47	  /**
    48	   * Maps intent prefixes to service areas.
    49	   */
    50	  const INTENT_AREA_MAP = [
    51	    'topic_housing' => 'housing',
    52	    'topic_family' => 'family',
    53	    'topic_seniors' => 'seniors',
    54	    'topic_health' => 'health',
    55	    'topic_consumer' => 'consumer',
    56	    'topic_civil_rights' => 'civil_rights',
    57	    'topic_employment' => 'civil_rights',
    58	    'topic_benefits' => 'health',
    59	  ];
    60	
    61	  /**
    62	   * Determines the fallback level based on context.
    63	   *
    64	   * @param string $intent
    65	   *   The current intent key.
    66	   * @param array $retrieval_results
    67	   *   Search results (empty means no results).
    68	   * @param array $server_history
    69	   *   Conversation history entries.
    70	   *
    71	   * @return int
    72	   *   Fallback level (1-4).
    73	   */
    74	  public static function determineLevel(string $intent, array $retrieval_results, array $server_history): int {
    75	    // Count consecutive fallback turns in recent history.
    76	    $fallback_count = self::countRecentFallbacks($server_history);
    77	
    78	    // Level 4: 3+ consecutive failures.
    79	    if ($fallback_count >= 3) {
    80	      return 4;
    81	    }
    82	
    83	    // Level 3: 2 failures.
    84	    if ($fallback_count >= 2) {
    85	      return 3;
    86	    }
    87	
    88	    // Level 2: repeated failure in same service area.
    89	    $area = self::resolveArea($intent);
    90	    if ($area && $fallback_count >= 1 && self::hasSameAreaInHistory($area, $server_history)) {
    91	      return 2;
    92	    }
    93	
    94	    // Level 1: first attempt with a known intent.
    95	    return 1;
    96	  }
    97	
    98	  /**
    99	   * Evaluates a fallback level and returns actionable response data.
   100	   *
   101	   * @param string $intent
   102	   *   The current intent key.
   103	   * @param array $retrieval_results
   104	   *   Search results.
   105	   * @param array $server_history
   106	   *   Conversation history.
   107	   * @param \Drupal\ilas_site_assistant\Service\TopIntentsPack|null $pack
   108	   *   Optional Top Intents Pack.
   109	   *
   110	   * @return array
   111	   *   Response data with keys:
   112	   *   - 'level' (int): The fallback level (1-4)
   113	   *   - 'message' (string): The fallback message
   114	   *   - 'primary_action' (array): {label, url}
   115	   *   - 'links' (array): Additional actionable links (>= 2 total)
   116	   *   - 'suggestions' (array): Chip suggestions (optional)
   117	   */
   118	  public static function evaluateLevel(string $intent, array $retrieval_results, array $server_history, ?TopIntentsPack $pack = NULL): array {
   119	    $level = self::determineLevel($intent, $retrieval_results, $server_history);
   120	    $area = self::resolveArea($intent);
   121	    $urls = self::DEFAULT_URLS;
   122	
   123	    switch ($level) {
   124	      case 1:
   125	        return self::buildLevel1($intent, $area, $pack, $urls);
   126	
   127	      case 2:
   128	        return self::buildLevel2($intent, $area, $pack, $urls);
   129	
   130	      case 3:
   131	        return self::buildLevel3($area, $pack, $urls);
   132	
   133	      case 4:
   134	      default:
   135	        return self::buildLevel4($urls);
   136	    }
   137	  }
   138	
   139	  /**
   140	   * Level 1: clarifier chips + contact secondary actions.
   141	   */
   142	  protected static function buildLevel1(string $intent, ?string $area, ?TopIntentsPack $pack, array $urls): array {
   143	    $suggestions = [];
   144	    $message = "I wasn't able to find a match for that. Let me help you narrow it down.";
   145	
   146	    // Try pack clarifier first.
   147	    if ($pack) {
   148	      $clarifier = $pack->getClarifier($intent);
   149	      if ($clarifier) {
   150	        $message = $clarifier['question'];
   151	        $suggestions = array_map(function ($opt) {
   152	          return ['label' => $opt['label'], 'action' => $opt['intent']];
   153	        }, $clarifier['options'] ?? []);
   154	      }
   155	      // Try chips as fallback.
   156	      elseif ($chips = $pack->getChips($intent)) {
   157	        $suggestions = array_map(function ($chip) {
   158	          return ['label' => $chip['label'], 'action' => $chip['intent']];
   159	        }, $chips);
   160	      }
   161	    }
   162	
   163	    $links = [
   164	      ['label' => 'Apply for Help', 'url' => $urls['apply'], 'type' => 'apply'],
   165	      ['label' => 'Call Legal Advice Line', 'url' => $urls['hotline'], 'type' => 'hotline'],
   166	    ];
   167	
   168	    // Add area-specific link if available.
   169	    if ($area && isset(self::SERVICE_AREA_URLS[$area])) {
   170	      array_unshift($links, [
   171	        'label' => ucfirst(str_replace('_', ' ', $area)) . ' Legal Help',
   172	        'url' => self::SERVICE_AREA_URLS[$area],
   173	        'type' => 'services',
   174	      ]);
   175	    }
   176	
   177	    return [
   178	      'level' => 1,
   179	      'message' => $message,
   180	      'primary_action' => ['label' => 'Apply for Help', 'url' => $urls['apply']],
   181	      'links' => $links,
   182	      'suggestions' => $suggestions,
   183	    ];
   184	  }
   185	
   186	  /**
   187	   * Level 2: parent service area page + nearby sub-topic chips.
   188	   */
   189	  protected static function buildLevel2(string $intent, ?string $area, ?TopIntentsPack $pack, array $urls): array {
   190	    $area_url = self::SERVICE_AREA_URLS[$area] ?? $urls['services'];
   191	    $area_label = $area ? ucfirst(str_replace('_', ' ', $area)) : 'Legal';
   192	
   193	    $message = "I'm having trouble finding exactly what you need. Here are some options for $area_label legal help.";
   194	
   195	    $suggestions = [];
   196	    if ($pack && $area) {
   197	      // Get chips from the parent area intent.
   198	      $parent_intent = 'topic_' . $area;
   199	      $chips = $pack->getChips($parent_intent);
   200	      if (!empty($chips)) {
   201	        $suggestions = array_map(function ($chip) {
   202	          return ['label' => $chip['label'], 'action' => $chip['intent']];
   203	        }, $chips);
   204	      }
   205	    }
   206	
   207	    return [
   208	      'level' => 2,
   209	      'message' => $message,
   210	      'primary_action' => ['label' => "$area_label Legal Help", 'url' => $area_url],
   211	      'links' => [
   212	        ['label' => "$area_label Legal Help", 'url' => $area_url, 'type' => 'services'],
   213	        ['label' => 'Find Forms', 'url' => $urls['forms'], 'type' => 'forms'],
   214	        ['label' => 'Apply for Help', 'url' => $urls['apply'], 'type' => 'apply'],
   215	        ['label' => 'Call Legal Advice Line', 'url' => $urls['hotline'], 'type' => 'hotline'],
   216	      ],
   217	      'suggestions' => $suggestions,
   218	    ];
   219	  }
   220	
   221	  /**
   222	   * Level 3: prominent contact info + 3-4 nearby intents.
   223	   */
   224	  protected static function buildLevel3(?string $area, ?TopIntentsPack $pack, array $urls): array {
   225	    $message = "I'm sorry I haven't been able to find what you need. Here are the best ways to get help directly.";
   226	
   227	    $suggestions = [
   228	      ['label' => 'Apply for Help', 'action' => 'apply_for_help'],
   229	      ['label' => 'Call Hotline', 'action' => 'legal_advice_line'],
   230	      ['label' => 'Find an Office', 'action' => 'offices_contact'],
   231	    ];
   232	
   233	    // Add area-specific chip if available.
   234	    if ($area) {
   235	      $suggestions[] = [
   236	        'label' => ucfirst(str_replace('_', ' ', $area)) . ' Help',
   237	        'action' => 'topic_' . $area,
   238	      ];
   239	    }
   240	
   241	    return [
   242	      'level' => 3,
   243	      'message' => $message,
   244	      'primary_action' => ['label' => 'Apply for Help', 'url' => $urls['apply']],
   245	      'links' => [
   246	        ['label' => 'Apply for Help', 'url' => $urls['apply'], 'type' => 'apply'],
   247	        ['label' => 'Call Legal Advice Line', 'url' => $urls['hotline'], 'type' => 'hotline'],
   248	        ['label' => 'Find an Office', 'url' => $urls['offices'], 'type' => 'offices'],
   249	        ['label' => 'Our Services', 'url' => $urls['services'], 'type' => 'services'],
   250	      ],
   251	      'suggestions' => $suggestions,
   252	    ];
   253	  }
   254	
   255	  /**
   256	   * Level 4: terminal — direct human connection.
   257	   */
   258	  protected static function buildLevel4(array $urls): array {
   259	    return [
   260	      'level' => 4,
   261	      'message' => "I can connect you with someone who can help directly. Please use one of the options below to reach us.",
   262	      'primary_action' => ['label' => 'Apply for Help', 'url' => $urls['apply']],
   263	      'links' => [
   264	        ['label' => 'Apply for Help', 'url' => $urls['apply'], 'type' => 'apply'],
   265	        ['label' => 'Call (208) 746-7541', 'url' => 'tel:208-746-7541', 'type' => 'hotline'],
   266	        ['label' => 'Find an Office', 'url' => $urls['offices'], 'type' => 'offices'],
   267	        ['label' => 'Our Services', 'url' => $urls['services'], 'type' => 'services'],
   268	      ],
   269	      'suggestions' => [
   270	        ['label' => 'Apply for Help', 'action' => 'apply_for_help'],
   271	        ['label' => 'Call Hotline', 'action' => 'legal_advice_line'],
   272	        ['label' => 'Find an Office', 'action' => 'offices_contact'],
   273	        ['label' => 'Our Services', 'action' => 'services_overview'],
   274	      ],
   275	    ];
   276	  }
   277	
   278	  /**
   279	   * Resolves an intent key to its parent service area.
   280	   *
   281	   * @param string $intent
   282	   *   The intent key.
   283	   *
   284	   * @return string|null
   285	   *   The service area key, or NULL.
   286	   */
   287	  public static function resolveArea(string $intent): ?string {
   288	    // Direct match.
   289	    if (isset(self::INTENT_AREA_MAP[$intent])) {
   290	      return self::INTENT_AREA_MAP[$intent];
   291	    }
   292	
   293	    // Check if intent starts with a known prefix.
   294	    foreach (self::INTENT_AREA_MAP as $prefix => $area) {
   295	      if (str_starts_with($intent, $prefix . '_')) {
   296	        return $area;
   297	      }
   298	    }
   299	
   300	    return NULL;
   301	  }
   302	
   303	  /**
   304	   * Counts consecutive fallback/unknown responses in recent history.
   305	   *
   306	   * @param array $server_history
   307	   *   Conversation history.
   308	   *
   309	   * @return int
   310	   *   Number of consecutive fallback turns from the end.
   311	   */
   312	  protected static function countRecentFallbacks(array $server_history): int {
   313	    $count = 0;
   314	    $fallback_types = ['fallback', 'unknown'];
   315	
   316	    for ($i = count($server_history) - 1; $i >= 0; $i--) {
   317	      $entry = $server_history[$i];
   318	      $response_type = $entry['response_type'] ?? '';
   319	      $intent = $entry['intent'] ?? '';
   320	
   321	      if (in_array($response_type, $fallback_types, TRUE) || $intent === 'unknown') {
   322	        $count++;
   323	      }
   324	      else {
   325	        break;
   326	      }
   327	    }
   328	
   329	    return $count;
   330	  }
   331	
   332	  /**
   333	   * Checks if any recent history entry shares the same service area.
   334	   *
   335	   * @param string $area
   336	   *   The current service area.
   337	   * @param array $server_history
   338	   *   Conversation history.
   339	   *
   340	   * @return bool
   341	   *   TRUE if a recent entry has the same area.
   342	   */
   343	  protected static function hasSameAreaInHistory(string $area, array $server_history): bool {
   344	    $recent = array_slice($server_history, -3);
   345	    foreach ($recent as $entry) {
   346	      if (($entry['area'] ?? '') === $area) {
   347	        return TRUE;
   348	      }
   349	    }
   350	    return FALSE;
   351	  }
   352	
   353	}

===== web/modules/custom/ilas_site_assistant/src/Service/SafetyViolationTracker.php =====
     1	<?php
     2	
     3	namespace Drupal\ilas_site_assistant\Service;
     4	
     5	use Drupal\Core\State\StateInterface;
     6	
     7	/**
     8	 * Ring-buffer timestamp tracker for sub-day safety violation counting.
     9	 *
    10	 * Stores violation timestamps in Drupal state to enable accurate
    11	 * sub-day windowed counting (e.g. "violations in the last hour").
    12	 * The date-bucketed stats table only supports day-level granularity;
    13	 * this tracker fills the gap for real-time alerting.
    14	 */
    15	class SafetyViolationTracker {
    16	
    17	  /**
    18	   * State key for the violation timestamp ring buffer.
    19	   */
    20	  const STATE_KEY = 'ilas_site_assistant.safety_violation_timestamps';
    21	
    22	  /**
    23	   * Maximum number of timestamps to retain in the ring buffer.
    24	   */
    25	  const MAX_ENTRIES = 500;
    26	
    27	  /**
    28	   * The state service.
    29	   *
    30	   * @var \Drupal\Core\State\StateInterface
    31	   */
    32	  protected $state;
    33	
    34	  /**
    35	   * Constructs a SafetyViolationTracker.
    36	   *
    37	   * @param \Drupal\Core\State\StateInterface $state
    38	   *   The state service.
    39	   */
    40	  public function __construct(StateInterface $state) {
    41	    $this->state = $state;
    42	  }
    43	
    44	  /**
    45	   * Records a safety violation at the given timestamp.
    46	   *
    47	   * @param int $timestamp
    48	   *   Unix timestamp of the violation.
    49	   */
    50	  public function record(int $timestamp): void {
    51	    $timestamps = $this->getTimestamps();
    52	    $timestamps[] = $timestamp;
    53	
    54	    // Trim to max entries, keeping the most recent.
    55	    if (count($timestamps) > self::MAX_ENTRIES) {
    56	      $timestamps = array_slice($timestamps, -self::MAX_ENTRIES);
    57	    }
    58	
    59	    $this->state->set(self::STATE_KEY, $timestamps);
    60	  }
    61	
    62	  /**
    63	   * Counts violations since the given cutoff timestamp.
    64	   *
    65	   * @param int $cutoff_timestamp
    66	   *   Unix timestamp; only violations at or after this time are counted.
    67	   *
    68	   * @return int
    69	   *   Number of violations since the cutoff.
    70	   */
    71	  public function countSince(int $cutoff_timestamp): int {
    72	    $timestamps = $this->getTimestamps();
    73	    $count = 0;
    74	    foreach ($timestamps as $ts) {
    75	      if ($ts >= $cutoff_timestamp) {
    76	        $count++;
    77	      }
    78	    }
    79	    return $count;
    80	  }
    81	
    82	  /**
    83	   * Removes timestamps older than the cutoff.
    84	   *
    85	   * Intended to be called from cron to prevent unbounded growth.
    86	   *
    87	   * @param int $cutoff_timestamp
    88	   *   Unix timestamp; entries before this are removed.
    89	   */
    90	  public function prune(int $cutoff_timestamp): void {
    91	    $timestamps = $this->getTimestamps();
    92	    $timestamps = array_values(array_filter($timestamps, fn($ts) => $ts >= $cutoff_timestamp));
    93	    $this->state->set(self::STATE_KEY, $timestamps);
    94	  }
    95	
    96	  /**
    97	   * Resets the tracker (for testing).
    98	   */
    99	  public function reset(): void {
   100	    $this->state->delete(self::STATE_KEY);
   101	  }
   102	
   103	  /**
   104	   * Gets the current timestamp ring buffer.
   105	   *
   106	   * @return int[]
   107	   *   Array of Unix timestamps.
   108	   */
   109	  protected function getTimestamps(): array {
   110	    return $this->state->get(self::STATE_KEY, []);
   111	  }
   112	
   113	}

===== web/modules/custom/ilas_site_assistant/src/Service/SafetyAlertService.php =====
     1	<?php
     2	
     3	namespace Drupal\ilas_site_assistant\Service;
     4	
     5	use Drupal\Core\Config\ConfigFactoryInterface;
     6	use Drupal\Core\Database\Connection;
     7	use Drupal\Core\Mail\MailManagerInterface;
     8	use Drupal\Core\State\StateInterface;
     9	use Drupal\Component\Datetime\TimeInterface;
    10	use Psr\Log\LoggerInterface;
    11	
    12	/**
    13	 * Cron-driven safety spike alerting service.
    14	 *
    15	 * Checks the ilas_site_assistant_stats table for safety_violation events
    16	 * that exceed a configurable threshold within a time window. Sends email
    17	 * alerts to configured recipients with no PII in the message body.
    18	 */
    19	class SafetyAlertService {
    20	
    21	  /**
    22	   * State key for tracking the last alert timestamp.
    23	   */
    24	  const STATE_LAST_ALERT = 'ilas_site_assistant.last_safety_alert';
    25	
    26	  /**
    27	   * The config factory.
    28	   *
    29	   * @var \Drupal\Core\Config\ConfigFactoryInterface
    30	   */
    31	  protected $configFactory;
    32	
    33	  /**
    34	   * The database connection.
    35	   *
    36	   * @var \Drupal\Core\Database\Connection
    37	   */
    38	  protected $database;
    39	
    40	  /**
    41	   * The mail manager.
    42	   *
    43	   * @var \Drupal\Core\Mail\MailManagerInterface
    44	   */
    45	  protected $mailManager;
    46	
    47	  /**
    48	   * The state service.
    49	   *
    50	   * @var \Drupal\Core\State\StateInterface
    51	   */
    52	  protected $state;
    53	
    54	  /**
    55	   * The time service.
    56	   *
    57	   * @var \Drupal\Component\Datetime\TimeInterface
    58	   */
    59	  protected $time;
    60	
    61	  /**
    62	   * The logger.
    63	   *
    64	   * @var \Psr\Log\LoggerInterface
    65	   */
    66	  protected $logger;
    67	
    68	  /**
    69	   * The violation tracker for sub-day windowed counting.
    70	   *
    71	   * @var \Drupal\ilas_site_assistant\Service\SafetyViolationTracker|null
    72	   */
    73	  protected $violationTracker;
    74	
    75	  /**
    76	   * Constructs a SafetyAlertService object.
    77	   */
    78	  public function __construct(
    79	    ConfigFactoryInterface $config_factory,
    80	    Connection $database,
    81	    MailManagerInterface $mail_manager,
    82	    StateInterface $state,
    83	    TimeInterface $time,
    84	    LoggerInterface $logger,
    85	    SafetyViolationTracker $violation_tracker = NULL
    86	  ) {
    87	    $this->configFactory = $config_factory;
    88	    $this->database = $database;
    89	    $this->mailManager = $mail_manager;
    90	    $this->state = $state;
    91	    $this->time = $time;
    92	    $this->logger = $logger;
    93	    $this->violationTracker = $violation_tracker;
    94	  }
    95	
    96	  /**
    97	   * Checks safety violation thresholds and sends alerts if exceeded.
    98	   *
    99	   * Called from hook_cron(). Respects cooldown period between alerts.
   100	   */
   101	  public function checkThresholds(): void {
   102	    $config = $this->configFactory->get('ilas_site_assistant.settings');
   103	
   104	    if (!$config->get('safety_alerting.enabled')) {
   105	      return;
   106	    }
   107	
   108	    $threshold = (int) ($config->get('safety_alerting.threshold') ?? 20);
   109	    $window_hours = (int) ($config->get('safety_alerting.window_hours') ?? 1);
   110	    $cooldown_minutes = (int) ($config->get('safety_alerting.cooldown_minutes') ?? 60);
   111	    $recipients = trim($config->get('safety_alerting.recipients') ?? '');
   112	
   113	    if (empty($recipients)) {
   114	      return;
   115	    }
   116	
   117	    // Check cooldown: don't send if we alerted recently.
   118	    $now = $this->time->getRequestTime();
   119	    $last_alert = (int) $this->state->get(self::STATE_LAST_ALERT, 0);
   120	    if ($last_alert > 0 && ($now - $last_alert) < ($cooldown_minutes * 60)) {
   121	      return;
   122	    }
   123	
   124	    // Count safety violations within the window.
   125	    // Prefer the violation tracker (accurate sub-day counting) over
   126	    // the stats table (date-bucketed, day-level granularity only).
   127	    $window_start_date = date('Y-m-d', $now - ($window_hours * 3600));
   128	    $today = date('Y-m-d', $now);
   129	
   130	    try {
   131	      if ($this->violationTracker) {
   132	        $cutoff = $now - ($window_hours * 3600);
   133	        $count = $this->violationTracker->countSince($cutoff);
   134	      }
   135	      else {
   136	        $count = $this->countSafetyViolations($window_start_date, $today);
   137	      }
   138	    }
   139	    catch (\Exception $e) {
   140	      $this->logger->error('Safety alert check failed: @message', [
   141	        '@message' => $e->getMessage(),
   142	      ]);
   143	      return;
   144	    }
   145	
   146	    if ($count < $threshold) {
   147	      return;
   148	    }
   149	
   150	    // Get top reason codes for the alert body (daily granularity acceptable).
   151	    $top_reasons = $this->getTopReasonCodes($window_start_date, $today, 5);
   152	
   153	    // Send the alert.
   154	    $this->sendAlert($recipients, $count, $window_hours, $top_reasons);
   155	
   156	    // Record that we sent an alert.
   157	    $this->state->set(self::STATE_LAST_ALERT, $now);
   158	
   159	    $this->logger->warning('Safety spike alert sent: @count violations in @hours hour(s). Recipients: @recipients', [
   160	      '@count' => $count,
   161	      '@hours' => $window_hours,
   162	      '@recipients' => $recipients,
   163	    ]);
   164	  }
   165	
   166	  /**
   167	   * Counts safety_violation events within a date range.
   168	   *
   169	   * @param string $start_date
   170	   *   Start date in Y-m-d format.
   171	   * @param string $end_date
   172	   *   End date in Y-m-d format.
   173	   *
   174	   * @return int
   175	   *   Total violation count.
   176	   */
   177	  protected function countSafetyViolations(string $start_date, string $end_date): int {
   178	    $query = $this->database->select('ilas_site_assistant_stats', 's')
   179	      ->condition('event_type', 'safety_violation')
   180	      ->condition('date', $start_date, '>=')
   181	      ->condition('date', $end_date, '<=');
   182	    $query->addExpression('SUM(s.count)', 'total');
   183	
   184	    $result = $query->execute()->fetchField();
   185	    return (int) ($result ?? 0);
   186	  }
   187	
   188	  /**
   189	   * Gets the top reason codes for safety violations in a date range.
   190	   *
   191	   * @param string $start_date
   192	   *   Start date in Y-m-d format.
   193	   * @param string $end_date
   194	   *   End date in Y-m-d format.
   195	   * @param int $limit
   196	   *   Maximum number of reason codes to return.
   197	   *
   198	   * @return array
   199	   *   Array of ['reason_code' => string, 'count' => int].
   200	   */
   201	  protected function getTopReasonCodes(string $start_date, string $end_date, int $limit): array {
   202	    $query = $this->database->select('ilas_site_assistant_stats', 's')
   203	      ->condition('event_type', 'safety_violation')
   204	      ->condition('date', $start_date, '>=')
   205	      ->condition('date', $end_date, '<=');
   206	    $query->addField('s', 'event_value', 'reason_code');
   207	    $query->addExpression('SUM(s.count)', 'total');
   208	    $query->groupBy('s.event_value');
   209	    $query->orderBy('total', 'DESC');
   210	    $query->range(0, $limit);
   211	
   212	    $results = [];
   213	    foreach ($query->execute() as $row) {
   214	      $results[] = [
   215	        'reason_code' => $row->reason_code,
   216	        'count' => (int) $row->total,
   217	      ];
   218	    }
   219	    return $results;
   220	  }
   221	
   222	  /**
   223	   * Sends the safety spike alert email.
   224	   *
   225	   * @param string $recipients
   226	   *   Comma-separated email addresses.
   227	   * @param int $count
   228	   *   Total violation count.
   229	   * @param int $window_hours
   230	   *   The lookback window in hours.
   231	   * @param array $top_reasons
   232	   *   Array of top reason codes with counts.
   233	   */
   234	  protected function sendAlert(string $recipients, int $count, int $window_hours, array $top_reasons): void {
   235	    $reason_lines = [];
   236	    foreach ($top_reasons as $reason) {
   237	      $reason_lines[] = '  - ' . $reason['reason_code'] . ' (' . $reason['count'] . ')';
   238	    }
   239	
   240	    $params = [
   241	      'subject' => '[ILAS Assistant] Safety spike alert',
   242	      'body' => implode("\n", [
   243	        'The ILAS Site Assistant has detected a spike in safety-classified messages.',
   244	        '',
   245	        'Violations detected: ' . $count,
   246	        'Time window: last ' . $window_hours . ' hour(s)',
   247	        'Date: ' . date('Y-m-d H:i T'),
   248	        '',
   249	        'Top reason codes:',
   250	        implode("\n", $reason_lines),
   251	        '',
   252	        'Review the full report at: /admin/reports/ilas-assistant',
   253	        'Review conversation logs at: /admin/reports/ilas-assistant/conversations',
   254	        '',
   255	        'This alert contains no personally identifiable information (PII).',
   256	        'This is an automated message from the ILAS Site Assistant module.',
   257	      ]),
   258	    ];
   259	
   260	    $addresses = array_map('trim', explode(',', $recipients));
   261	    foreach ($addresses as $address) {
   262	      if (!filter_var($address, FILTER_VALIDATE_EMAIL)) {
