  1169	
  1170	    /**
  1171	     * Add a message to the chat.
  1172	     */
  1173	    addMessage: function (sender, content, isHtml) {
  1174	      isHtml = isHtml || false;
  1175	      var chat = this.isPageMode
  1176	        ? document.getElementById('assistant-chat')
  1177	        : this.widget.querySelector('.assistant-chat');
  1178	
  1179	      if (!chat) return;
  1180	
  1181	      var messageEl = document.createElement('div');
  1182	      messageEl.className = 'chat-message chat-message--' + sender;
  1183	
  1184	      var contentEl = document.createElement('div');
  1185	      contentEl.className = 'message-content';
  1186	
  1187	      if (isHtml) {
  1188	        contentEl.innerHTML = content;
  1189	      } else {
  1190	        contentEl.textContent = content;
  1191	      }
  1192	
  1193	      messageEl.appendChild(contentEl);
  1194	
  1195	      // Insert message before the sentinel so it stays at the end.
  1196	      if (this.scrollManager && this.scrollManager.sentinel) {
  1197	        chat.insertBefore(messageEl, this.scrollManager.sentinel);
  1198	      } else {
  1199	        chat.appendChild(messageEl);
  1200	      }
  1201	
  1202	      // Conditionally scroll: only if user is near bottom.
  1203	      // Pass the message element so the scroll manager can detect tall
  1204	      // messages and scroll to their top instead of the container bottom.
  1205	      if (this.scrollManager) {
  1206	        this.scrollManager.onNewContent(messageEl);
  1207	      } else {
  1208	        // Fallback for edge case where scroll manager isn't ready.
  1209	        chat.scrollTop = chat.scrollHeight;
  1210	      }
  1211	
  1212	      // Store in history (text only).
  1213	      if (sender === 'user') {
  1214	        this.messageHistory.push({ role: 'user', content: typeof content === 'string' ? content : '' });
  1215	      }
  1216	
  1217	      // Bind events to new inline buttons.
  1218	      var self = this;
  1219	      messageEl.querySelectorAll('.inline-suggestion-btn, .topic-suggestion-btn').forEach(function (btn) {
  1220	        btn.addEventListener('click', function (e) {
  1221	          self.handleQuickAction(e.currentTarget.dataset.action);
  1222	        });
  1223	      });
  1224	
  1225	      // Bind tracking to new links.
  1226	      messageEl.querySelectorAll('[data-assistant-track]').forEach(function (link) {
  1227	        link.addEventListener('click', function (e) {
  1228	          var trackType = e.currentTarget.dataset.assistantTrack;
  1229	          self.trackClick(trackType, e.currentTarget.href);
  1230	        });
  1231	      });
  1232	    },
  1233	
  1234	    /**
  1235	     * Show typing indicator with ARIA status for screen readers.
  1236	     */
  1237	    showTyping: function () {
  1238	      var chat = this.isPageMode
  1239	        ? document.getElementById('assistant-chat')
  1240	        : this.widget.querySelector('.assistant-chat');
  1241	
  1242	      var typing = document.createElement('div');
  1243	      typing.className = 'chat-message chat-message--assistant chat-message--typing';
  1244	      typing.id = 'typing-indicator';
  1245	      typing.setAttribute('role', 'status');
  1246	      typing.setAttribute('aria-label', Drupal.t('Aila is typing'));
  1247	      typing.innerHTML = '<div class="typing-indicator" aria-hidden="true">' +
  1248	        '<span></span><span></span><span></span>' +
  1249	        '</div>';
  1250	
  1251	      // Insert before sentinel so it stays at the end.
  1252	      if (this.scrollManager && this.scrollManager.sentinel) {
  1253	        chat.insertBefore(typing, this.scrollManager.sentinel);
  1254	      } else {
  1255	        chat.appendChild(typing);
  1256	      }
  1257	
  1258	      // Conditionally scroll.
  1259	      if (this.scrollManager) {
  1260	        this.scrollManager.onNewContent(typing);
  1261	      } else {
  1262	        chat.scrollTop = chat.scrollHeight;
  1263	      }
  1264	    },
  1265	
  1266	    /**
  1267	     * Hide typing indicator.
  1268	     */
  1269	    hideTyping: function () {
  1270	      var typing = document.getElementById('typing-indicator');
  1271	      if (typing) {
  1272	        typing.remove();
  1273	      }
  1274	    },
  1275	
  1276	    /**
  1277	     * Call the API with AbortController timeout and status-aware errors.
  1278	     *
  1279	     * @param {string} endpoint - API path (appended to config.apiBase).
  1280	     * @param {Object} options - fetch options (method, body, etc.).
  1281	     * @return {Promise} Resolves with parsed JSON; rejects with enriched Error
  1282	     *   containing .type ('offline'|'timeout') or .status (HTTP code) and
  1283	     *   optionally .retryAfter (seconds from Retry-After header).
  1284	     */
  1285	    callApi: function (endpoint, options) {
  1286	      options = options || {};
  1287	      var url = this.config.apiBase + endpoint;
  1288	
  1289	      // Offline check.
  1290	      if (typeof navigator !== 'undefined' && navigator.onLine === false) {
  1291	        var offlineErr = new Error('Offline');
  1292	        offlineErr.type = 'offline';
  1293	        return Promise.reject(offlineErr);
  1294	      }
  1295	
  1296	      // AbortController for timeout.
  1297	      var controller = new AbortController();
  1298	      var timeoutMs = options.timeout || 15000;
  1299	      var timeoutId = setTimeout(function () {
  1300	        controller.abort();
  1301	      }, timeoutMs);
  1302	
  1303	      var defaultOptions = {
  1304	        method: 'GET',
  1305	        headers: {
  1306	          'Content-Type': 'application/json',
  1307	          'X-CSRF-Token': this.config.csrfToken,
  1308	        },
  1309	        credentials: 'same-origin',
  1310	        signal: controller.signal,
  1311	      };
  1312	
  1313	      // Merge options (excluding our custom timeout key).
  1314	      var fetchOptions = Object.assign({}, defaultOptions, options);
  1315	      delete fetchOptions.timeout;
  1316	
  1317	      return fetch(url, fetchOptions)
  1318	        .then(function (response) {
  1319	          clearTimeout(timeoutId);
  1320	          if (!response.ok) {
  1321	            var error = new Error('API request failed: ' + response.status);
  1322	            error.status = response.status;
  1323	            if (response.status === 429) {
  1324	              var retryHeader = response.headers.get('Retry-After');
  1325	              if (retryHeader) {
  1326	                error.retryAfter = retryHeader;
  1327	              }
  1328	            }
  1329	            throw error;
  1330	          }
  1331	          return response.json();
  1332	        })
  1333	        .catch(function (error) {
  1334	          clearTimeout(timeoutId);
  1335	          if (error.name === 'AbortError') {
  1336	            var timeoutError = new Error('Request timed out');
  1337	            timeoutError.type = 'timeout';
  1338	            throw timeoutError;
  1339	          }
  1340	          throw error;
  1341	        });
  1342	    },
  1343	
  1344	    /**
  1345	     * Return a user-friendly error message based on error type / HTTP status.
  1346	     *
  1347	     * @param {Error} error - The error from callApi.
  1348	     * @return {string} Translated message suitable for addMessage().
  1349	     */
  1350	    getErrorMessage: function (error) {
  1351	      if (!error) {
  1352	        return Drupal.t('Something went wrong. Please try again.');
  1353	      }
  1354	
  1355	      // Offline.
  1356	      if (error.type === 'offline') {
  1357	        return Drupal.t('You appear to be offline. Please check your connection and try again.');
  1358	      }
  1359	
  1360	      // Timeout.
  1361	      if (error.type === 'timeout') {
  1362	        return Drupal.t('The request took too long. Please try again.');
  1363	      }
  1364	
  1365	      // 429 Too Many Requests.
  1366	      if (error.status === 429) {
  1367	        var msg = Drupal.t('I\'m getting a lot of requests right now.');
  1368	        if (error.retryAfter) {
  1369	          var seconds = parseInt(error.retryAfter, 10);
  1370	          if (!isNaN(seconds) && seconds > 0) {
  1371	            msg += ' ' + Drupal.t('Please wait @seconds seconds and try again.', { '@seconds': seconds });
  1372	          } else {
  1373	            msg += ' ' + Drupal.t('Please wait a moment and try again.');
  1374	          }
  1375	        } else {
  1376	          msg += ' ' + Drupal.t('Please wait a moment and try again.');
  1377	        }
  1378	        return msg;
  1379	      }
  1380	
  1381	      // 403 Forbidden.
  1382	      if (error.status === 403) {
  1383	        return Drupal.t('Access denied. Please refresh the page and try again.');
  1384	      }
  1385	
  1386	      // 5xx Server errors.
  1387	      if (error.status >= 500) {
  1388	        return Drupal.t('Our server is having trouble right now. Please try again in a few minutes, or reach us through our hotline.');
  1389	      }
  1390	
  1391	      // Generic fallback.
  1392	      return Drupal.t('I\'m having trouble right now. You can try again, or reach us directly through our hotline.');
  1393	    },
  1394	
  1395	    /**
  1396	     * Toggle the sending-disabled state on send button and input.
  1397	     *
  1398	     * @param {boolean} sending - True to disable, false to re-enable.
  1399	     */
  1400	    setSendingState: function (sending) {
  1401	      var btns;
  1402	      if (this.isPageMode) {
  1403	        btns = document.querySelectorAll('.assistant-send-btn');
  1404	      } else if (this.widget) {
  1405	        btns = this.widget.querySelectorAll('.assistant-send-btn');
  1406	      }
  1407	      if (btns) {
  1408	        btns.forEach(function (btn) {
  1409	          btn.disabled = sending;
  1410	        });
  1411	      }
  1412	      if (this.inputField) {
  1413	        this.inputField.disabled = sending;
  1414	      }
  1415	    },
  1416	
  1417	    /**
  1418	     * Track an event.
  1419	     */
  1420	    trackEvent: function (eventType, eventValue) {
  1421	      eventValue = eventValue || '';
  1422	      // Push to dataLayer if available.
  1423	      if (window.dataLayer) {
  1424	        window.dataLayer.push({
  1425	          event: 'aila_chat_' + eventType,
  1426	          event_category: 'Aila Chat',
  1427	          event_action: eventType,
  1428	          event_label: eventValue,
  1429	        });
  1430	      }
  1431	
  1432	      // Also log to server.
  1433	      this.callApi('/track', {
  1434	        method: 'POST',
  1435	        body: JSON.stringify({
  1436	          event_type: eventType,
  1437	          event_value: eventValue,
  1438	        }),
  1439	      }).catch(function () {
  1440	        // Silent fail for tracking.
  1441	      });
  1442	    },
  1443	
  1444	    /**
  1445	     * Track a click event.
  1446	     */
  1447	    trackClick: function (eventType, url) {
  1448	      // Extract path from URL.
  1449	      var path = url;
  1450	      try {
  1451	        var urlObj = new URL(url, window.location.origin);
  1452	        path = urlObj.pathname;
  1453	      } catch (e) {
  1454	        // Use as-is if URL parsing fails.
  1455	      }
  1456	
  1457	      this.trackEvent(eventType, path);
  1458	    },
  1459	
  1460	    /**
  1461	     * Escape HTML to prevent XSS in element content.
  1462	     */
  1463	    escapeHtml: function (text) {
  1464	      if (!text) return '';
  1465	      var div = document.createElement('div');
  1466	      div.textContent = text;
  1467	      return div.innerHTML;
  1468	    },
  1469	
  1470	    /**
  1471	     * Escape a string for safe use in an HTML attribute value.
  1472	     *
  1473	     * Covers &, ", ', <, > — the five characters that can break out of
  1474	     * a quoted attribute context.
  1475	     *
  1476	     * @param {string} text - Raw string.
  1477	     * @return {string} Attribute-safe string.
  1478	     */
  1479	    escapeAttr: function (text) {
  1480	      if (!text || typeof text !== 'string') return '';
  1481	      return text
  1482	        .replace(/&/g, '&amp;')
  1483	        .replace(/"/g, '&quot;')
  1484	        .replace(/'/g, '&#39;')
  1485	        .replace(/</g, '&lt;')
  1486	        .replace(/>/g, '&gt;');
  1487	    },
  1488	
  1489	    /**
  1490	     * Validate and sanitize a URL, blocking dangerous schemes.
  1491	     *
  1492	     * Allows http:, https:, mailto:, tel:, and relative paths (/ or #).
  1493	     * Returns '#' for anything else (javascript:, data:, vbscript:, etc.).
  1494	     *
  1495	     * @param {string} url - Raw URL from API response or config.
  1496	     * @return {string} Sanitized URL or '#'.
  1497	     */
  1498	    sanitizeUrl: function (url) {
  1499	      if (!url || typeof url !== 'string') return '#';
  1500	
  1501	      var trimmed = url.trim();
  1502	      if (!trimmed) return '#';
  1503	
  1504	      // Allow relative paths.
  1505	      if (trimmed.charAt(0) === '/') return trimmed;
  1506	      // Allow fragment-only URLs.
  1507	      if (trimmed.charAt(0) === '#') return trimmed;
  1508	
  1509	      // Validate scheme via URL parser.
  1510	      try {
  1511	        var parsed = new URL(trimmed, window.location.origin);
  1512	        var scheme = parsed.protocol.toLowerCase();
  1513	        if (scheme === 'http:' || scheme === 'https:' || scheme === 'mailto:' || scheme === 'tel:') {
  1514	          return trimmed;
  1515	        }
  1516	      } catch (e) {
  1517	        // URL parsing failed — reject.
  1518	      }
  1519	
  1520	      return '#';
  1521	    },
  1522	  };
  1523	
  1524	  /**
  1525	   * Drupal behavior.
  1526	   */
  1527	  Drupal.behaviors.ilasSiteAssistant = {
  1528	    attach: function (context, settings) {
  1529	      if (!settings.ilasSiteAssistant) return;
  1530	
  1531	      once('ilas-site-assistant', 'body', context).forEach(function () {
  1532	        SiteAssistant.init(settings.ilasSiteAssistant);
  1533	      });
  1534	    },
  1535	  };
  1536	
  1537	})(Drupal, drupalSettings, once);

    75	  cache_ttl: 3600  # Response cache TTL in seconds (0 = disabled)

    90	# When enabled, stores PII-redacted message pairs for QA/debugging.
    91	conversation_logging:

    93	  retention_hours: 72         # Auto-delete after this many hours
    94	  redact_pii: true            # Apply PII redaction before storing
    95	  show_user_notice: true      # Display notice in widget about review
    96	
    97	# Fallback Gate Configuration
    98	# Controls when to use built-in answers vs LLM (Gemini) fallback
    99	fallback_gate:
   100	  thresholds:
   101	    # Intent confidence thresholds (0-1)
   102	    intent_high_conf: 0.85       # High confidence = answer directly
   103	    intent_medium_conf: 0.65     # Medium confidence = answer with lower certainty
   104	    intent_low_conf: 0.40        # Below this = consider fallback
   105	
   106	    # Retrieval score thresholds (0-1)
   107	    retrieval_high_score: 0.75   # High retrieval = confident answer
   108	    retrieval_medium_score: 0.50 # Medium retrieval = may need enhancement
   109	    retrieval_low_score: 0.30    # Below this = low confidence in results
   110	
   111	    # Score gap thresholds (indicates clear winner in results)
   112	    retrieval_score_gap_high: 0.25  # Large gap = clear best result
   113	    retrieval_score_gap_low: 0.10   # Small gap = ambiguous results
   114	
   115	    # Minimum results needed
   116	    retrieval_min_results: 1
   117	
   118	    # Combined confidence thresholds
   119	    combined_high_conf: 0.80         # High combined = confident answer
   120	    combined_fallback_threshold: 0.50 # Below this = consider LLM fallback
   121	
   122	    # Message characteristics
   123	    short_message_words: 3      # Messages this short get lower confidence
   124	    ambiguous_message_words: 2  # Very short = ask for clarification
   125	
   126	# Vector Search Enhancement (Option A: fallback enrichment)
   127	# When lexical results are sparse, supplements with Pinecone semantic search.
   128	# Requires pinecone_vector Search API server and vector indexes to be configured.
   129	#
   130	# Score normalization notes:
   131	# - Cosine similarity scores range 0-1 (higher = more similar).
   132	# - Lexical (BM25) scores are on a different, unbounded scale.
   133	# - score_normalization_factor converts cosine to a comparable range.
   134	#   Default 100 means cosine 0.72 becomes score 72.
   135	# - Since vector search only fires when lexical results are sparse,
   136	#   the normalization primarily affects ordering within the merged set.
   137	# - The raw cosine score is preserved as 'vector_score' on each result
   138	#   for operator tuning and debugging.
   139	# - The Pinecone HTTP client uses Guzzle defaults (~30s timeout).
   140	#   Vector search failures are caught and return empty results gracefully.
   141	vector_search:
   142	  enabled: false
   143	  faq_index_id: 'faq_accordion_vector'
   144	  resource_index_id: 'assistant_resources_vector'
   145	  fallback_threshold: 2        # Min lexical results before vector fires
   146	  min_vector_score: 0.70       # Min cosine similarity to include (0-1)
   147	  score_normalization_factor: 100  # Multiply cosine score by this for ranking parity
   148	  min_lexical_score: 0         # Min lexical score for quality check (0 = disabled)
   149	                               # If set > 0 and all lexical results score below this,
   150	                               # vector search fires even when count >= fallback_threshold
   151	
   152	# Safety spike alerting (cron-driven, default OFF)
   153	# Sends email when safety violations exceed threshold within window.
   154	safety_alerting:
   155	  enabled: false
   156	  threshold: 20               # Number of violations to trigger alert
   157	  window_hours: 1             # Lookback window in hours
   158	  cooldown_minutes: 60        # Minimum gap between alerts
   159	  recipients: ''              # Comma-separated email addresses
   160	
   161	# History-aware intent fallback (fires ONLY on direct-route unknown)
   162	# When the router returns 'unknown', checks recent conversation history
   163	# for a dominant topic to maintain multi-turn continuity.
   164	history_fallback:
   165	  enabled: true
   166	  max_turns: 6
   167	  time_window_sec: 600
   168	  dominance_threshold: 0.5
   169	  min_eligible_turns: 1
   170	
   171	# A/B testing framework (minimal, default OFF)
   172	# Deterministic variant assignment per conversation_id.
   173	ab_testing:
   174	  enabled: false
   175	  experiments:
   176	    - id: 'welcome_v2'
   177	      variants:
   178	        - 'control'
   179	        - 'expanded_welcome'
   180	      allocation:
   181	        - 50
   182	        - 50
   183	
   184	# Langfuse observability (default OFF)
   185	# Credentials should be set via settings.php from environment variables:
   186	#   $config['ilas_site_assistant.settings']['langfuse']['public_key'] = getenv('LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY');
   187	#   $config['ilas_site_assistant.settings']['langfuse']['secret_key'] = getenv('LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY');
   188	#   $config['ilas_site_assistant.settings']['langfuse']['environment'] = getenv('PANTHEON_ENVIRONMENT') ?: 'local';
   189	langfuse:
   190	  enabled: false
   191	  host: 'https://us.cloud.langfuse.com'
   192	  public_key: ''
   193	  secret_key: ''
   194	  sample_rate: 1.0
   195	  environment: 'production'

===== web/sites/default/settings.php =====
     1	<?php
     2	
     3	/**
     4	 * SECURITY: Early Exit for Spam & Scrapers (Added Nov 30, 2025)
     5	 * Prevents database exhaustion from facet spam and known bot patterns.
     6	 * Placed at the top to exit before bootstrapping Drupal.
     7	 */
     8	if (isset($_SERVER['REQUEST_URI']) &&
     9	    strpos($_SERVER['REQUEST_URI'], '/admin/') === false &&
    10	    (strpos($_SERVER['REQUEST_URI'], '/search') === 0 ||
    11	     strpos($_SERVER['REQUEST_URI'], '/search?') === 0 ||
    12	     preg_match('#^/[^/]+/search($|\?)#', $_SERVER['REQUEST_URI']))) {
    13	
    14	  // 1. Block ALL Facet Parameters (The "Kill Switch")
    15	  // Faceted search is not enabled on this site (Facets module disabled).
    16	  // Any request with f[...] parameters is bot traffic.
    17	  // Updated Dec 19, 2025: Changed from blocking f[5]+ to blocking ALL facet params
    18	  // to address China-based botnet attack using 5-filter combinations.
    19	  if (isset($_SERVER['QUERY_STRING']) && (strpos($_SERVER['QUERY_STRING'], 'f[') !== false || strpos($_SERVER['QUERY_STRING'], 'f%5B') !== false)) {
    20	      header('HTTP/1.1 429 Too Many Requests');
    21	      die('Error: Invalid search parameters.');
    22	  }
    23	
    24	}
    25	
    26	/**
    27	 * SECURITY: Block direct access to core utility scripts (Added Feb 11, 2026).
    28	 *
    29	 * Prevents information disclosure from install.php / rebuild.php stack traces
    30	 * and settings.php 200-OK responses. Must run before settings.pantheon.php.
    31	 * Skips CLI so drush/terminus remain unaffected.
    32	 */
    33	if (PHP_SAPI !== 'cli' && isset($_SERVER['SCRIPT_NAME'])) {
    34	  $blocked_scripts = [
    35	    '/core/install.php',
    36	    '/core/rebuild.php',
    37	    '/sites/default/settings.php',
    38	  ];
    39	  foreach ($blocked_scripts as $script) {
    40	    if (strpos($_SERVER['SCRIPT_NAME'], $script) === 0) {
    41	      header('HTTP/1.1 403 Forbidden');
    42	      die('Access denied.');
    43	    }
    44	  }
    45	}
    46	
    47	/**
    48	 * SECURITY: Block core documentation files that disclose Drupal version (L-2).
    49	 *
    50	 * These text files are served as static assets by nginx/Apache before Drupal
    51	 * bootstraps, so they must be blocked here in settings.php (early enough for
    52	 * Pantheon's infrastructure). REQUEST_URI is used because SCRIPT_NAME always
    53	 * points to index.php for routed requests.
    54	 */
    55	if (PHP_SAPI !== 'cli' && isset($_SERVER['REQUEST_URI'])) {
    56	  $request_path = strtok($_SERVER['REQUEST_URI'], '?');
    57	  if (preg_match('#^/core/(CHANGELOG|COPYRIGHT|INSTALL|LICENSE|MAINTAINERS|UPDATE)\.txt$#i', $request_path)) {
    58	    header('HTTP/1.1 404 Not Found');
    59	    die('Not found.');
    60	  }
    61	}
    62	
    63	/**
    64	 * Load services definition file.
    65	 */
    66	$settings['container_yamls'][] = __DIR__ . '/services.yml';
    67	
    68	/**
    69	 * Include the Pantheon-specific settings file.
    70	 *
    71	 * n.b. The settings.pantheon.php file makes some changes
    72	 * that affect all environments that this site
    73	 * exists in.  Always include this file, even in
    74	 * a local development environment, to ensure that
    75	 * the site settings remain consistent.
    76	 */
    77	include __DIR__ . "/settings.pantheon.php";
    78	
    79	/**
    80	 * Permissions-Policy header.
    81	 *
    82	 * Restricts browser features not needed by this site. Uses the modern
    83	 * Permissions-Policy format (not the deprecated Feature-Policy).
    84	 * SecKit's feature_policy setting is intentionally left disabled because
    85	 * it sends the deprecated Feature-Policy header name.
    86	 *
    87	 * See: Finding M-13 in security audit (Feb 2026).
    88	 */
    89	if (PHP_SAPI !== 'cli') {
    90	  header('Permissions-Policy: attribution-reporting=(self "https://www.google-analytics.com" "https://analytics.google.com" "https://www.googletagmanager.com" "https://www.google.com" "https://www.googleadservices.com" "https://googleads.g.doubleclick.net"), camera=(), microphone=(), geolocation=(), payment=(), usb=(), bluetooth=(), accelerometer=(), gyroscope=(), magnetometer=()');
    91	}
    92	
    93	/**
    94	 * Google Analytics (GA4) tracking ID for Google tag (gtag.js).
    95	 * Only enable this on the live Pantheon environment.
    96	 */
    97	if (isset($_ENV['PANTHEON_ENVIRONMENT']) && $_ENV['PANTHEON_ENVIRONMENT'] === 'live') {
    98	  $settings['google_tag_id'] = 'G-QYT2ZNY442';
    99	
   100	  // Production rate limits for the chatbot API (per IP).
   101	  $config['ilas_site_assistant.settings']['rate_limit_per_minute'] = 15;
   102	  $config['ilas_site_assistant.settings']['rate_limit_per_hour'] = 120;
   103	}
   104	
   105	/**
   106	 * Skipping permissions hardening will make scaffolding
   107	 * work better, but will also raise a warning when you
   108	 * install Drupal.
   109	 *
   110	 * https://www.drupal.org/project/drupal/issues/3091285
   111	 */
   112	// $settings['skip_permissions_hardening'] = TRUE;
   113	
   114	
   115	/**
   116	 * Helper: retrieve a secret value.
   117	 *
   118	 * On Pantheon: uses pantheon_get_secret() (natively available on the platform).
   119	 *   Secrets MUST be type "runtime", scope "web".
   120	 * Locally (DDEV): falls back to getenv(), so you can set values in .ddev/.env.
   121	 *
   122	 * @param string $name
   123	 *   The secret name (same key used in Pantheon Dashboard and in .ddev/.env).
   124	 *
   125	 * @return string|false
   126	 *   The secret value, or FALSE if not set.
   127	 */
   128	function _ilas_get_secret(string $name) {
   129	  // Pantheon runtime secrets (type: runtime, scope: web).
   130	  if (function_exists('pantheon_get_secret')) {
   131	    $val = pantheon_get_secret($name);
   132	    if ($val !== FALSE && $val !== '') {
   133	      return $val;
   134	    }
   135	  }
   136	  // Local / DDEV fallback: read from environment variable.
   137	  return getenv($name);
   138	}
   139	
   140	/**
   141	 * SMTP password override.
   142	 *
   143	 * On Pantheon: type "runtime", scope "web", key "SMTP_PASSWORD".
   144	 * Locally (DDEV): add SMTP_PASSWORD=<value> to .ddev/.env, then ddev restart.
   145	 */
   146	$smtp_pass = _ilas_get_secret('SMTP_PASSWORD');
   147	if ($smtp_pass) {
   148	  $config['symfony_mailer_lite.symfony_mailer_lite_transport.smtp']['configuration']['pass'] = $smtp_pass;
   149	}
   150	
   151	/**
   152	 * Cloudflare Turnstile keys override.
   153	 *
   154	 * The Turnstile module stores its site key and secret key inside the Key
   155	 * module's config entity "cloudflare_turnstile_keys". The config provider is
   156	 * "config", which reads from key_provider_settings.key_value (a JSON string).
   157	 * By overriding that JSON at runtime, `drush cim` cannot wipe the keys.
   158	 *
   159	 * On Pantheon: type "runtime", scope "web":
   160	 *   TURNSTILE_SITE_KEY
   161	 *   TURNSTILE_SECRET_KEY
   162	 * Locally (DDEV): add to .ddev/.env, then ddev restart.
   163	 */
   164	$turnstile_site_key   = _ilas_get_secret('TURNSTILE_SITE_KEY');
   165	$turnstile_secret_key = _ilas_get_secret('TURNSTILE_SECRET_KEY');
   166	if ($turnstile_site_key || $turnstile_secret_key) {
   167	  $config['key.key.cloudflare_turnstile_keys']['key_provider_settings']['key_value'] = json_encode([
   168	    'site_key'   => $turnstile_site_key ?: '',
   169	    'secret_key' => $turnstile_secret_key ?: '',
   170	  ]);
   171	}
   172	
   173	/**
   174	 * TMGMT Google Translate API key override.
   175	 *
   176	 * On Pantheon: type "runtime", scope "web", key "TMGMT_GOOGLE_API_KEY".
   177	 * Locally (DDEV): add TMGMT_GOOGLE_API_KEY=<value> to .ddev/.env, then ddev restart.
   178	 */
   179	$tmgmt_google_key = _ilas_get_secret('TMGMT_GOOGLE_API_KEY');
   180	if ($tmgmt_google_key) {
   181	  $config['tmgmt.translator.google']['settings']['api_key'] = $tmgmt_google_key;
   182	}
   183	
   184	/**
   185	 * ILAS Site Assistant Gemini API key override.
   186	 *
   187	 * On Pantheon: type "runtime", scope "web", key "ILAS_GEMINI_API_KEY".
   188	 * Locally (DDEV): add ILAS_GEMINI_API_KEY=<value> to .ddev/.env, then ddev restart.
   189	 */
   190	$ilas_gemini_key = _ilas_get_secret('ILAS_GEMINI_API_KEY');
   191	if ($ilas_gemini_key) {
   192	  $config['ilas_site_assistant.settings']['llm.api_key'] = $ilas_gemini_key;
   193	}
   194	
   195	/**
   196	 * ILAS Site Assistant Vertex AI service account JSON override.
   197	 *
   198	 * On Pantheon: type "runtime", scope "web", key "ILAS_VERTEX_SA_JSON".
   199	 * Locally (DDEV): add ILAS_VERTEX_SA_JSON=<json> to .ddev/.env, then ddev restart.
   200	 */
   201	$ilas_vertex_sa = _ilas_get_secret('ILAS_VERTEX_SA_JSON');
   202	if ($ilas_vertex_sa) {
   203	  $config['ilas_site_assistant.settings']['llm.service_account_json'] = $ilas_vertex_sa;
   204	}
   205	
   206	/**
   207	 * Langfuse observability API keys.
   208	 *
   209	 * On Pantheon: type "runtime", scope "web", keys "LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY" and "LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY".
   210	 * Locally (DDEV): add to .ddev/.env, then ddev restart.
   211	 */
   212	$langfuse_pk = _ilas_get_secret('LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY');
   213	$langfuse_sk = _ilas_get_secret('LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY');
   214	if ($langfuse_pk && $langfuse_sk) {
   215	  $config['ilas_site_assistant.settings']['langfuse']['public_key'] = $langfuse_pk;
   216	  $config['ilas_site_assistant.settings']['langfuse']['secret_key'] = $langfuse_sk;
   217	}
   218	// Set environment label from Pantheon environment.
   219	if (defined('PANTHEON_ENVIRONMENT')) {
   220	  $config['ilas_site_assistant.settings']['langfuse']['environment'] = PANTHEON_ENVIRONMENT;
   221	}
   222	
   223	/**
   224	 * Gemini API key for Drupal AI module (Google AI Studio).
   225	 * Key entity "gemini_api_key" (config provider, empty default).
   226	 * Reuses the same secret as ilas_site_assistant above.
   227	 */
   228	$gemini_for_ai = _ilas_get_secret('ILAS_GEMINI_API_KEY');
   229	if ($gemini_for_ai) {
   230	  $config['key.key.gemini_api_key']['key_provider_settings']['key_value'] = $gemini_for_ai;
   231	}
   232	
   233	/**
   234	 * Vertex AI credentials for Drupal AI module (fallback provider).
   235	 * Key entity "vertex_sa_credentials" (config provider, empty default).
   236	 * Reuses the same SA JSON secret as ilas_site_assistant above.
   237	 */
   238	$vertex_sa_for_ai = _ilas_get_secret('ILAS_VERTEX_SA_JSON');
   239	if ($vertex_sa_for_ai) {
   240	  $config['key.key.vertex_sa_credentials']['key_provider_settings']['key_value'] = $vertex_sa_for_ai;
   241	}
   242	
   243	/**
   244	 * Pinecone API key for AI Search vector database.
   245	 * Key entity "pinecone_api_key" (config provider, empty default).
   246	 *
   247	 * On Pantheon: type "runtime", scope "web", key "ILAS_PINECONE_API_KEY".
   248	 * Locally (DDEV): add ILAS_PINECONE_API_KEY=<value> to .ddev/.env, then ddev restart.
   249	 */
   250	$pinecone_key = _ilas_get_secret('ILAS_PINECONE_API_KEY');
   251	if ($pinecone_key) {
   252	  $config['key.key.pinecone_api_key']['key_provider_settings']['key_value'] = $pinecone_key;
   253	}
   254	
   255	/**
   256	 * Sentry error tracking via drupal/raven.
   257	 *
   258	 * On Pantheon: type "runtime", scope "web", key "SENTRY_DSN".
   259	 * Locally (DDEV): add SENTRY_DSN=<value> to .ddev/.env, then ddev restart.
   260	 */
   261	$sentry_dsn = _ilas_get_secret('SENTRY_DSN');
   262	if ($sentry_dsn) {
   263	  $config['raven.settings']['client_key'] = $sentry_dsn;
   264	  $config['raven.settings']['environment'] = getenv('PANTHEON_ENVIRONMENT') ?: 'local';
   265	  $config['raven.settings']['release'] = getenv('PANTHEON_DEPLOYMENT_IDENTIFIER') ?: NULL;
   266	  $config['raven.settings']['log_levels'] = [
   267	    'emergency' => TRUE,
   268	    'alert' => TRUE,
   269	    'critical' => TRUE,
   270	    'error' => TRUE,
   271	    'warning' => FALSE,
   272	    'notice' => FALSE,
   273	    'info' => FALSE,
   274	    'debug' => FALSE,
   275	  ];
   276	}
   277	
   278	/**
   279	 * Include DDEV settings if present.
   280	 * Safe: this file doesn't exist on Pantheon.
   281	 */
   282	$ddev_settings = __DIR__ . '/settings.ddev.php';
   283	if (is_readable($ddev_settings)) {
   284	  require $ddev_settings;
   285	}
   286	
   287	/**
   288	 * Include local settings LAST so it can override DDEV.
   289	 */
   290	$local_settings = __DIR__ . '/settings.local.php';
   291	if (file_exists($local_settings)) {
   292	  include $local_settings;
   293	}

===== web/sites/default/settings.pantheon.php =====
     1	<?php
     2	
     3	// phpcs:ignoreFile
     4	
     5	/**
     6	 * @file
     7	 * Pantheon configuration file.
     8	 *
     9	 * IMPORTANT NOTE:
    10	 * Do not modify this file. This file is maintained by Pantheon.
    11	 *
    12	 * Site-specific modifications belong in settings.php, not this file. This file
    13	 * may change in future releases and modifications would cause conflicts when
    14	 * attempting to apply upstream updates.
    15	 */
    16	
    17	/**
    18	 * Version of Pantheon files.
    19	 *
    20	 * This is a monotonically-increasing sequence number.
    21	 */
    22	if (!defined("PANTHEON_VERSION")) {
    23	  define("PANTHEON_VERSION", "4");
    24	}
    25	
    26	/**
    27	 * Determine whether this is a preproduction or production environment, and
    28	 * then load the pantheon services.yml file.  This file should be named either
    29	 * 'pantheon-production-services.yml' (for 'live' or 'test' environments)
    30	 * 'pantheon-preproduction-services.yml' (for 'dev' or multidev environments).
    31	 */
    32	$pantheon_services_file = __DIR__ . '/services.pantheon.preproduction.yml';
    33	if (
    34	  isset($_ENV['PANTHEON_ENVIRONMENT']) &&
    35	  ( ($_ENV['PANTHEON_ENVIRONMENT'] == 'live') || ($_ENV['PANTHEON_ENVIRONMENT'] == 'test') )
    36	) {
    37	  $pantheon_services_file = __DIR__ . '/services.pantheon.production.yml';
    38	}
    39	
    40	if (file_exists($pantheon_services_file)) {
    41	  $settings['container_yamls'][] = $pantheon_services_file;
    42	}
    43	
    44	/**
    45	 * Set the default location for the 'private' directory.  Note
    46	 * that this location is protected when running on the Pantheon
    47	 * environment, but may be exposed if you migrate your site to
    48	 * another environment.
    49	 */
    50	$settings['file_private_path'] = 'sites/default/files/private';
    51	
    52	// Check to see if we are serving an installer page.
    53	$is_installer_url = (strpos($_SERVER['SCRIPT_NAME'], '/core/install.php') === 0);
    54	
    55	/**
    56	 * Add the Drupal 8 CMI Directory Information directly in settings.php to make sure
    57	 * Drupal knows all about that.
    58	 *
    59	 * Issue: https://github.com/pantheon-systems/drops-8/issues/2
    60	 *
    61	 * IMPORTANT SECURITY NOTE:  The configuration paths set up
    62	 * below are secure when running your site on Pantheon.  If you
    63	 * migrate your site to another environment on the public internet,
    64	 * you should relocate these locations. See "After Installation"
    65	 * at https://www.drupal.org/node/2431247
    66	 *
    67	 */
    68	if (empty($settings['config_sync_directory'])) {
    69	  if ($is_installer_url) {
    70	    $settings['config_sync_directory'] = 'sites/default/files';
    71	  } else {
    72	    $settings['config_sync_directory'] = getenv('DOCROOT') ? '../config' : 'sites/default/config';
    73	  }
    74	}
    75	
    76	
    77	/**
    78	 * Allow Drupal 8 to Cleanly Redirect to Install.php For New Sites.
    79	 *
    80	 * Issue: https://github.com/pantheon-systems/drops-8/issues/3
    81	 *
    82	 * c.f. https://github.com/pantheon-systems/drops-8/pull/53
    83	 *
    84	 */
    85	if (
    86	  isset($_ENV['PANTHEON_ENVIRONMENT']) &&
    87	  !$is_installer_url &&
    88	  (isset($_SERVER['PANTHEON_DATABASE_STATE']) && ($_SERVER['PANTHEON_DATABASE_STATE'] == 'empty')) &&
    89	  (empty($GLOBALS['install_state'])) &&
    90	  (php_sapi_name() != "cli") &&
    91	  ($_ENV['PANTHEON_ENVIRONMENT'] !== "test") &&
    92	  ($_ENV['PANTHEON_ENVIRONMENT'] !== "live")
    93	) {
    94	  include_once __DIR__ . '/../../core/includes/install.core.inc';
    95	  include_once __DIR__ . '/../../core/includes/install.inc';
    96	  install_goto('core/install.php');
    97	}
    98	
    99	/**
   100	 * Override the $databases variable to pass the correct Database credentials
   101	 * directly from Pantheon to Drupal.
   102	 *
   103	 * Issue: https://github.com/pantheon-systems/drops-8/issues/8
   104	 *
   105	 */
   106	if (isset($_SERVER['PRESSFLOW_SETTINGS'])) {
   107	  $pressflow_settings = json_decode($_SERVER['PRESSFLOW_SETTINGS'], TRUE);
   108	  foreach ($pressflow_settings as $key => $value) {
   109	    // One level of depth should be enough for $conf and $database.
   110	    if ($key == 'conf') {
   111	      foreach($value as $conf_key => $conf_value) {
   112	        $conf[$conf_key] = $conf_value;
   113	      }
   114	    }
   115	    elseif ($key == 'databases') {
   116	      // Protect default configuration but allow the specification of
   117	      // additional databases. Also, allows fun things with 'prefix' if they
   118	      // want to try multisite.
   119	      if (!isset($databases) || !is_array($databases)) {
   120	        $databases = array();
   121	      }
   122	      $databases = array_replace_recursive($databases, $value);
   123	    }
   124	    else {
   125	      $$key = $value;
   126	    }
   127	  }
   128	}
   129	
   130	/**
   131	 * Handle Hash Salt Value from Drupal
   132	 *
   133	 * Changing these will invalidate all one-time login links.
   134	 * Pantheon sets this values for you. If you want to shuffle it you could
   135	 * use terminus env:rotate-random-seed command:
   136	 * https://docs.pantheon.io/terminus/commands/env-rotate-random-seed
   137	 *
   138	 */
   139	if (isset($_ENV['PANTHEON_ENVIRONMENT'])) {
   140	  $settings['hash_salt'] = $_ENV['DRUPAL_HASH_SALT'];
   141	}
   142	
   143	/**
   144	 * Define appropriate location for tmp directory
   145	 *
   146	 * Issue: https://github.com/pantheon-systems/drops-8/issues/114
   147	 *
   148	 */
   149	if (isset($_ENV['PANTHEON_ENVIRONMENT'])) {
   150	  $settings["file_temp_path"] = sys_get_temp_dir();
   151	}
   152	
   153	/**
   154	 * Place Twig cache files in the Pantheon rolling temporary directory.
   155	 * A new rolling temporary directory is provided on every code deploy,
   156	 * guaranteeing that fresh twig cache files will be generated every time.
   157	 * Note that the rendered output generated from the twig cache files
   158	 * are also cached in the database, so a cache clear is still necessary
   159	 * to see updated results after a code deploy.
   160	 */
   161	if (isset($_ENV['PANTHEON_ROLLING_TMP']) && isset($_ENV['PANTHEON_DEPLOYMENT_IDENTIFIER'])) {
   162	  // Relocate the compiled twig files to <binding-dir>/tmp/ROLLING/twig.
   163	  // The location of ROLLING will change with every deploy.
   164	  $settings['php_storage']['twig']['directory'] = $_ENV['PANTHEON_ROLLING_TMP'];
   165	  // Ensure that the compiled twig templates will be rebuilt whenever the
   166	  // deployment identifier changes.  Note that a cache rebuild is also necessary.
   167	  $settings['deployment_identifier'] = $_ENV['PANTHEON_DEPLOYMENT_IDENTIFIER'];
   168	  $settings['php_storage']['twig']['secret'] = $_ENV['DRUPAL_HASH_SALT'] . $settings['deployment_identifier'];
   169	}
   170	
   171	/**
   172	 * Install the Pantheon Service Provider to hook Pantheon services into
   173	 * Drupal 11. This service provider handles operations such as clearing the
   174	 * Pantheon edge cache whenever the Drupal cache is rebuilt.
   175	 */
   176	if (isset($_ENV['PANTHEON_ENVIRONMENT'])) {
   177	  $GLOBALS['conf']['container_service_providers']['PantheonServiceProvider'] = '\Pantheon\Internal\PantheonServiceProvider11';
   178	}
   179	
   180	/**
   181	 * "Trusted host settings" are not necessary on Pantheon; traffic will only
   182	 * be routed to your site if the host settings match a domain configured for
   183	 * your site in the dashboard.
   184	 */
   185	if (isset($_ENV['PANTHEON_ENVIRONMENT'])) {
   186	  $settings['trusted_host_patterns'][] = '.*';
   187	}
   188	
   189	/**
   190	 * The default list of directories that will be ignored by Drupal's file API.
   191	 *
   192	 * By default ignore node_modules and bower_components folders to avoid issues
   193	 * with common frontend tools and recursive scanning of directories looking for
   194	 * extensions.
   195	 *
   196	 * @see file_scan_directory()
   197	 * @see \Drupal\Core\Extension\ExtensionDiscovery::scanDirectory()
   198	 */
   199	if (empty($settings['file_scan_ignore_directories'])) {
   200	  $settings['file_scan_ignore_directories'] = [
   201	    'node_modules',
   202	    'bower_components',
   203	  ];
   204	}

===== web/themes/custom/b5subtheme/scss/style.scss =====
     1	// =============================================================================

===== promptfoo-evals/promptfooconfig.yaml =====
     1	description: "ILAS Site Assistant — LIVE multi-turn evals"
     2	
     3	prompts:
     4	  - file://prompts/ilas-thread.yaml
     5	
     6	providers:
     7	  - file://providers/ilas-live.js
     8	
     9	defaultTest:
    10	  assert:
    11	    - type: javascript
    12	      value: "output.length > 50"
    13	      metric: non-empty-response
    14	    - type: javascript
    15	      value: "!output.toLowerCase().includes('error') && !output.toLowerCase().includes('failed')"
    16	      metric: no-error-keywords
    17	    - type: javascript
    18	      value: "const l = output.toLowerCase(); l.includes('not legal advice') || l.includes('informational') || l.includes('disclaimer') || l.includes('cannot provide legal advice') || l.includes(\"can't give legal advice\") || l.includes('general information') || l.includes('not a substitute')"
    19	      metric: includes-caveat
    20	
    21	tests:
    22	  - file://tests/live-10convos.yaml

===== promptfoo-evals/providers/ilas-live.js =====
     1	/**
     2	 * ILAS Live Assistant — Promptfoo custom provider
     3	 *
     4	 * Handles Drupal CSRF token acquisition and POSTs questions to the
     5	 * live assistant endpoint at /assistant/api/message.
     6	 *
     7	 * Environment:
     8	 *   ILAS_ASSISTANT_URL — full URL to the message endpoint
     9	 *     e.g. https://idaholegalaid.org/assistant/api/message
    10	 *   ILAS_SITE_BASE_URL — optional base for turning relative URLs into absolute
    11	 *     default: https://idaholegalaid.org
    12	 *   ILAS_REQUEST_DELAY_MS — minimum ms between requests (default: 0)
    13	 *     Set to 31000 for live site (120/hour limit → ~2/min pacing)
    14	 *     Set to 0 for local DDEV (no rate limits)
    15	 *   ILAS_429_MAX_RETRIES — max retries on 429 (default: 5)
    16	 *   ILAS_429_BASE_WAIT_MS — base wait on first 429 retry (default: 65000)
    17	 *
    18	 * No external dependencies — uses Node.js 18+ built-in fetch and crypto.
    19	 */
    20	
    21	const crypto = require('node:crypto');
    22	
    23	// --- Rate-limit pacing ---
    24	const REQUEST_DELAY_MS = parseInt(process.env.ILAS_REQUEST_DELAY_MS || '0', 10);
    25	const MAX_429_RETRIES = parseInt(process.env.ILAS_429_MAX_RETRIES || '5', 10);
    26	const BASE_429_WAIT_MS = parseInt(process.env.ILAS_429_BASE_WAIT_MS || '65000', 10);
    27	
    28	/** Shared timestamp of last successful request (across all provider instances). */
    29	let lastRequestTime = 0;
    30	let requestCount = 0;
    31	
    32	function sleep(ms) {
    33	  return new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, ms));
    34	}
    35	
    36	/** Wait until the minimum inter-request delay has elapsed. */
    37	async function paceRequest() {
    38	  if (REQUEST_DELAY_MS <= 0) return;
    39	  const now = Date.now();
    40	  const elapsed = now - lastRequestTime;
    41	  if (elapsed < REQUEST_DELAY_MS) {
    42	    const waitMs = REQUEST_DELAY_MS - elapsed;
    43	    await sleep(waitMs);
    44	  }
    45	  lastRequestTime = Date.now();
    46	}
    47	
    48	const DEFAULT_SITE_BASE_URL = 'https://idaholegalaid.org';
    49	const SITE_BASE_URL = process.env.ILAS_SITE_BASE_URL || DEFAULT_SITE_BASE_URL;
    50	
    51	function toAbsoluteUrl(u) {
    52	  if (!u) return u;
    53	
    54	  // Already absolute?
    55	  try {
    56	    // eslint-disable-next-line no-new
    57	    new URL(u);
    58	    return u;
    59	  } catch (_) {
    60	    // Relative -> absolute
    61	    try {
    62	      return new URL(u, SITE_BASE_URL).toString();
    63	    } catch (e) {
    64	      // If it's not a valid URL at all, return original string
    65	      return u;
    66	    }
    67	  }
    68	}
    69	
    70	/**
    71	 * Convert any "/relative/path" occurrences inside arbitrary text to absolute URLs.
    72	 * This is what stops Promptfoo from trying to "sanitize" relative paths and
    73	 * complaining that they're invalid URLs.
    74	 */
    75	function absolutizeRelativePathsInText(text) {
    76	  if (!text || typeof text !== 'string') return text;
    77	
    78	  // Replace occurrences of /something (not //something) that are word-ish paths.
    79	  // We keep the preceding delimiter (space, punctuation, start-of-string) so
    80	  // we don't glue words together.
    81	  //
    82	  // Examples converted:
    83	  //   "Apply here: /apply-for-help" -> "Apply here: https://idaholegalaid.org/apply-for-help"
    84	  //   "(See /services.)" -> "(See https://idaholegalaid.org/services.)"
    85	  //
    86	  // Not converted:
    87	  //   "//cdn.example.com" (protocol-relative)
    88	  //   "/ " (bare slash)
    89	  return text.replace(
    90	    /(^|[\s([{"'`])\/(?!\/)([A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9/_\-]*)(?=($|[\s)\]}",'`.!?;:]))/g,
    91	    (match, prefix, path) => `${prefix}${toAbsoluteUrl('/' + path)}`
    92	  );
    93	}
    94	
    95	function extractFirstSetCookieValue(setCookieHeader) {
    96	  // Node fetch returns a single "set-cookie" header string (often only one cookie in our case).
    97	  // We only need the first cookie key=value portion before ';'.
    98	  if (!setCookieHeader) return null;
    99	  const first = setCookieHeader.split('\n')[0] || setCookieHeader;
   100	  const cookiePair = first.split(';')[0]?.trim();
   101	  return cookiePair || null;
   102	}
   103	
   104	class IlasLiveProvider {
   105	  constructor(options = {}) {
   106	    this.providerId = options.id || 'ilas-live';
   107	    this.baseUrl = null;
   108	    this.messageUrl = null;
   109	    this.csrfToken = null;
   110	
   111	    // For anonymous session continuity (Drupal CSRF tokens are session-scoped)
   112	    this.cookie = null;
   113	  }
   114	
   115	  id() {
   116	    return this.providerId;
   117	  }
   118	
   119	  /** Derive base URL and message URL from the environment variable. */
   120	  _resolveUrls() {
   121	    const envUrl = process.env.ILAS_ASSISTANT_URL;
   122	    if (!envUrl) {
   123	      throw new Error(
   124	        'ILAS_ASSISTANT_URL is not set. ' +
   125	          'Export it before running, e.g.: export ILAS_ASSISTANT_URL=https://idaholegalaid.org/assistant/api/message'
   126	      );
   127	    }
   128	    const parsed = new URL(envUrl);
   129	    this.baseUrl = `${parsed.protocol}//${parsed.host}`;
   130	    this.messageUrl = envUrl;
   131	  }
   132	
   133	  /** Fetch a fresh CSRF token from /session/token (captures anonymous session cookie). */
   134	  async _fetchCsrfToken() {
   135	    const tokenUrl = `${this.baseUrl}/session/token`;
   136	    const res = await fetch(tokenUrl, {
   137	      method: 'GET',
   138	      headers: { Accept: 'text/plain' },
   139	    });
   140	
   141	    if (!res.ok) {
   142	      throw new Error(`CSRF token fetch failed: ${res.status} ${res.statusText}`);
   143	    }
   144	
   145	    // Capture session cookie if present (common for Drupal)
   146	    const setCookie = res.headers.get('set-cookie');
   147	    const cookiePair = extractFirstSetCookieValue(setCookie);
   148	    if (cookiePair) {
   149	      this.cookie = cookiePair;
   150	    }
   151	
   152	    this.csrfToken = (await res.text()).trim();
   153	  }
   154	
   155	  _getConversationId(prompt, context) {
   156	    // Try common locations promptfoo may pass metadata/vars in.
   157	    const v = context?.vars || {};
   158	    const m =
   159	      context?.metadata ||
   160	      context?.test?.metadata ||
   161	      context?.testCase?.metadata ||
   162	      v?.metadata ||
   163	      {};
   164	
   165	    return (
   166	      m.conversationId ||
   167	      v.conversation_id ||
   168	      v.conversationId ||
   169	      crypto.randomUUID()
   170	    );
   171	  }
   172	
   173	  async callApi(prompt, context) {
   174	    // Lazy-init URLs on first call.
   175	    if (!this.messageUrl) {
   176	      this._resolveUrls();
   177	    }
   178	
   179	    // Fetch CSRF token on first call.
   180	    if (!this.csrfToken) {
   181	      await this._fetchCsrfToken();
   182	    }
   183	
   184	    // Use the raw question variable (not the rendered chat prompt).
   185	    const question = context?.vars?.question || prompt;
   186	
   187	    const conversationId = this._getConversationId(prompt, context);
   188	
   189	    // Use vars.history if present (multi-turn), otherwise start fresh.
   190	    const priorHistory = Array.isArray(context?.vars?.history)
   191	      ? context.vars.history
   192	      : [];
   193	    const history = [...priorHistory, { role: 'user', content: question }];
   194	
   195	    const body = JSON.stringify({
   196	      message: question,
   197	      conversation_id: conversationId,
   198	      context: { history },
   199	    });
   200	
   201	    // --- Pace request to respect rate limits ---
   202	    await paceRequest();
   203	
   204	    const headers = {
   205	      'Content-Type': 'application/json',
   206	      Accept: 'application/json',
   207	      'X-CSRF-Token': this.csrfToken,
   208	    };
   209	
   210	    // If we captured an anonymous session cookie from /session/token, send it
   211	    if (this.cookie) {
   212	      headers.Cookie = this.cookie;
   213	    }
   214	
   215	    let res;
   216	    let attempt = 0;
   217	
   218	    // eslint-disable-next-line no-constant-condition
   219	    while (true) {
   220	      try {
   221	        res = await fetch(this.messageUrl, { method: 'POST', headers, body });
   222	      } catch (err) {
   223	        return { error: `Network error: ${err?.message || String(err)}` };
   224	      }
   225	
   226	      // On 403 (CSRF expired), refresh token and retry once.
   227	      if (res.status === 403 && attempt === 0) {
   228	        try {
   229	          await this._fetchCsrfToken();
   230	          headers['X-CSRF-Token'] = this.csrfToken;
   231	          if (this.cookie) headers.Cookie = this.cookie;
   232	          res = await fetch(this.messageUrl, { method: 'POST', headers, body });
   233	        } catch (retryErr) {
   234	          return { error: `CSRF retry failed: ${retryErr?.message || String(retryErr)}` };
   235	        }
   236	      }
   237	
   238	      // Rate limit — backoff and retry.
   239	      if (res.status === 429) {
   240	        attempt++;
   241	        if (attempt > MAX_429_RETRIES) {
   242	          return { error: `Rate limited (429) after ${MAX_429_RETRIES} retries. Giving up.` };
   243	        }
   244	        const retryAfter = res.headers.get('Retry-After');
   245	        const waitMs = retryAfter
   246	          ? parseInt(retryAfter, 10) * 1000
   247	          : BASE_429_WAIT_MS * Math.pow(1.5, attempt - 1);
   248	        requestCount++;
   249	        const shortQ = question.length > 40 ? question.slice(0, 40) + '...' : question;
   250	        console.log(
   251	          `[${requestCount}] 429 on "${shortQ}" — retry ${attempt}/${MAX_429_RETRIES}, waiting ${Math.round(waitMs / 1000)}s`
   252	        );
   253	        await sleep(waitMs);
   254	        lastRequestTime = Date.now();
   255	        continue;
   256	      }
   257	
   258	      break; // Not a 429 — proceed
   259	    }
   260	
   261	    // Other HTTP errors.
   262	    if (!res.ok) {
   263	      const text = await res.text().catch(() => '');
   264	      return { error: `HTTP ${res.status}: ${text.slice(0, 300)}` };
   265	    }
   266	
   267	    // Log progress.
   268	    requestCount++;
   269	    const shortQ = question.length > 50 ? question.slice(0, 50) + '...' : question;
   270	    const meta = context?.test?.metadata || context?.metadata || {};
   271	    const turnInfo = meta.turn && meta.totalTurns ? ` T${meta.turn}/${meta.totalTurns}` : '';
   272	    const convoName = meta.conversationName || '';
   273	    console.log(`[${requestCount}/292] ${convoName}${turnInfo} — "${shortQ}"`);
   274	
   275	
   276	    let data;
   277	    try {
   278	      data = await res.json();
   279	    } catch (err) {
   280	      const text = await res.text().catch(() => '');
   281	      return {
   282	        error: `Invalid JSON response: ${err?.message || String(err)} — ${text.slice(0, 200)}`,
   283	      };
   284	    }
   285	
   286	    // Build rich output: message + result titles/answers + actions + caveat.
   287	    // The assistant returns structured data across multiple fields; concatenating
   288	    // them gives assertions access to the full response content.
   289	    const parts = [];
   290	
   291	    if (data.message) {
   292	      parts.push(absolutizeRelativePathsInText(data.message));
   293	    }
   294	
   295	    if (data.url) {
   296	      parts.push(`[Navigate: ${toAbsoluteUrl(data.url)}]`);
   297	    }
   298	
   299	    if (Array.isArray(data.results)) {
   300	      for (const r of data.results) {
   301	        if (r.title) parts.push(absolutizeRelativePathsInText(`Result: ${r.title}`));
   302	        if (r.question && r.question !== r.title) {
   303	          parts.push(absolutizeRelativePathsInText(`Q: ${r.question}`));
   304	        }
   305	        if (r.answer) parts.push(absolutizeRelativePathsInText(r.answer));
   306	        if (r.url) parts.push(`[Result URL: ${toAbsoluteUrl(r.url)}]`);
   307	      }
   308	    }
   309	
   310	    if (Array.isArray(data.secondary_actions)) {
   311	      for (const a of data.secondary_actions) {
   312	        if (a.label) {
   313	          parts.push(`Action: ${absolutizeRelativePathsInText(a.label)} (${toAbsoluteUrl(a.url)})`);
   314	        }
   315	      }
   316	    }
   317	
   318	    if (Array.isArray(data.links)) {
   319	      for (const link of data.links) {
   320	        if (link.label) {
   321	          parts.push(`${absolutizeRelativePathsInText(link.label)} (${toAbsoluteUrl(link.url)})`);
   322	        }
   323	      }
   324	    }
   325	
   326	    if (data.primary_action?.label) {
   327	      parts.push(
   328	        `Primary: ${absolutizeRelativePathsInText(data.primary_action.label)} (${toAbsoluteUrl(
   329	          data.primary_action.url
   330	        )})`
   331	      );
   332	    }
   333	
   334	    if (data.caveat) {
   335	      parts.push(absolutizeRelativePathsInText(data.caveat));
   336	    }
   337	
   338	    // Final safety pass (catches any stray relative paths in concatenated output)
   339	    const output = absolutizeRelativePathsInText(parts.join('\n\n')) || JSON.stringify(data);
   340	
   341	    return {
   342	      output,
   343	      tokenUsage: {},
   344	    };
   345	  }
   346	}
   347	
   348	module.exports = IlasLiveProvider;

===== promptfoo-evals/README.md =====
     1	# ILAS Site Assistant — Promptfoo Evaluation Harness
     2	
     3	Evaluation scaffolding for the Idaho Legal Aid Services site assistant.
     4	Uses [Promptfoo](https://promptfoo.dev) for multi-turn conversation evaluation
     5	with simulated-user support.
     6	
     7	## Status
     8	
     9	**Live-wired.** The default provider is the custom JS provider at
    10	`providers/ilas-live.js`, which handles Drupal CSRF tokens automatically.
    11	Run `npm run eval:promptfoo:live` to evaluate against the production assistant.
    12	
    13	## Directory layout
    14	
    15	```
    16	promptfoo-evals/
    17	├── promptfooconfig.yaml          # Main config — providers, tests, options
    18	├── package.json                  # js-yaml dependency for generator script
    19	├── providers/
    20	│   └── ilas-live.js              # Custom provider (CSRF + live endpoint + multi-turn)
    21	├── prompts/
    22	│   └── ilas-thread.yaml          # Chat prompt template (Nunjucks + YAML)
    23	├── tests/
    24	│   ├── live-50.sanitized.yaml    # 50 single-turn source questions
    25	│   ├── live-10convos.yaml        # Generated: 10 convos x 5 turns (from generator)
    26	│   └── simulated-user-smoke.yaml # Smoke test suite (5 tests)
    27	├── scripts/
    28	│   ├── generate-multiturn.js     # Converts 50 single-turn → 10 multi-turn convos
    29	│   ├── run-promptfoo.sh          # Bash wrapper (Linux/macOS)
    30	│   └── run-promptfoo.ps1         # PowerShell wrapper (Windows)
    31	├── output/                       # Generated results (git-ignored)
    32	└── README.md                     # This file
    33	```
    34	
    35	## Quick start
    36	
    37	### Prerequisites
    38	
    39	- Node.js >= 18 (for `npx`)
    40	- No global install needed — scripts use `npx promptfoo@latest`
    41	
    42	### Run evaluation against LIVE endpoint
    43	
    44	From the **repo root**:
    45	
    46	```bash
    47	npm run eval:promptfoo:live
    48	```
    49	
    50	This sets `ILAS_ASSISTANT_URL` inline and runs the 5-test smoke suite against
    51	the production assistant. The custom provider at `providers/ilas-live.js`
    52	automatically fetches a Drupal CSRF token before the first request and retries
    53	once on 403 (token expiry).
    54	
    55	**Safety notes:**
    56	- Keep the test set small (5 tests at concurrency 1 = ~5 requests)
    57	- Do not run simulated-user/redteam against production — use a Pantheon multidev
    58	- No PII in test questions
    59	- Rate limits: 15 req/min, 120 req/hr per IP
    60	
    61	To run manually or against a different environment:
    62	
    63	```bash
    64	export ILAS_ASSISTANT_URL=https://your-multidev.idaholegalaid.org/assistant/api/message
    65	bash promptfoo-evals/scripts/run-promptfoo.sh eval
    66	```
    67	
    68	### Run evaluation (dry-run / offline)
    69	
    70	To run offline without hitting the live endpoint, edit `promptfooconfig.yaml`
    71	to uncomment the `echo` provider and comment out the `file://` provider:
    72	
    73	```bash
    74	# Linux / macOS
    75	npm run eval:promptfoo
    76	
    77	# Windows PowerShell
    78	npm run eval:promptfoo:win
    79	```
    80	
    81	### View results
    82	
    83	```bash
    84	npm run view:promptfoo
    85	```
    86	
    87	This starts the Promptfoo web UI as a **foreground process**. The viewer
    88	auto-selects port 15500 (or the next free port up to 15510). The URL is only
    89	accessible while the process is running — press `Ctrl+C` to stop it.
    90	
    91	To use a custom starting port:
    92	
    93	```bash
    94	PROMPTFOO_PORT=16000 npm run view:promptfoo
    95	```
    96	
    97	## Privacy & offline defaults
    98	
    99	All wrapper scripts automatically set these environment variables:
   100	
   101	| Variable                             | Value  | Purpose                          |
   102	|--------------------------------------|--------|----------------------------------|
   103	| `PROMPTFOO_DISABLE_TELEMETRY`        | `1`    | No usage analytics               |
   104	| `PROMPTFOO_DISABLE_UPDATE`           | `1`    | No update checks                 |
   105	| `PROMPTFOO_DISABLE_REMOTE_GENERATION`| `true` | No remote test generation        |
   106	| `PROMPTFOO_DISABLE_SHARING`          | `1`    | No result sharing                |
   107	| `PROMPTFOO_SELF_HOSTED`             | `1`    | Self-hosted mode                 |
   108	
   109	You can also add these to a `.env` file in `promptfoo-evals/` or export them
   110	in your shell profile.
   111	
   112	## How the custom provider works
   113	
   114	The custom provider (`providers/ilas-live.js`) handles the Drupal assistant
   115	API contract:
   116	
   117	1. **CSRF token** — on first request, fetches a token from
   118	   `${baseUrl}/session/token` (derived from `ILAS_ASSISTANT_URL`)
   119	2. **POST** — sends `{"message": "<question>", "conversation_id": "<uuid>"}` to
   120	   `/assistant/api/message` with the token in `X-CSRF-Token` header
   121	3. **Response** — extracts `response.message` as the output
   122	4. **Error handling** — retries once on 403 (CSRF expiry), reports 429 rate
   123	   limits, returns descriptive errors for network failures
   124	
   125	No cookies or authentication needed — the endpoint accepts anonymous sessions.
   126	
   127	### Enable simulated-user multi-turn (optional)
   128	
   129	Uncomment the `redteam` block in `promptfooconfig.yaml` and set
   130	`OPENAI_API_KEY` (or another LLM provider key) so the simulated user can
   131	drive conversations. Only run this against a Pantheon multidev, not production.
   132	
   133	## Multi-turn test generation
   134	
   135	The default eval suite uses 10 multi-turn conversations (5 turns each, 50 tests
   136	total) to detect quality degradation as conversation context grows.
   137	
   138	### One-time setup
   139	
   140	```bash
   141	cd promptfoo-evals && npm install && cd ..
   142	```
   143	
   144	### Generate multi-turn tests
   145	
   146	```bash
   147	npm run generate:multiturn --prefix promptfoo-evals
   148	# or: node promptfoo-evals/scripts/generate-multiturn.js
   149	```
   150	
   151	This reads `tests/live-50.sanitized.yaml` (50 single-turn questions), splits
   152	them into 10 sequential groups of 5, and writes `tests/live-10convos.yaml`.
   153	
   154	Each turn carries the prior user messages in `vars.history`, which the custom
   155	provider sends as `context.history` in the API request body. Turn 1 of each
   156	conversation has an empty history; turn 5 has 4 prior messages.
   157	
   158	Re-run the generator after editing the source questions in
   159	`live-50.sanitized.yaml`.
   160	
   161	## Adding new test suites
   162	
   163	Create YAML files in `tests/` and reference them in `promptfooconfig.yaml`:
   164	
   165	```yaml
   166	tests:
   167	  - tests/simulated-user-smoke.yaml
   168	  - tests/your-new-suite.yaml
   169	```
   170	
   171	Each test case should include:
   172	- `vars.question` — the user message
   173	- `vars.goal` — conversation goal (for documentation / simulated user)
   174	- `assert` — one or more assertions
   175	- `metadata.conversationId` — groups turns in the same conversation
   176	
   177	## Troubleshooting
   178	
   179	- **"Cannot find module promptfoo"** — ensure Node.js >= 18 is installed.
   180	  Scripts use `npx promptfoo@latest` so no global install is needed.
   181	- **Network errors** — the default `echo` provider makes no network calls.
   182	  If you see connection errors, you may have uncommented the HTTP provider
   183	  without setting `ILAS_ASSISTANT_URL`.
   184	- **Telemetry warnings** — the wrapper scripts disable telemetry by default.
   185	  If running `npx promptfoo` directly, set the env vars listed above.
   186	
   187	### Viewer troubleshooting (WSL2)
   188	
   189	- **Viewer lifecycle** — the URL (`http://localhost:<PORT>`) only works while
   190	  `npm run view:promptfoo` is running. Deep-link URLs (e.g. `/eval-...`)
   191	  require the viewer to be running with access to that eval's data; always
   192	  start from the root URL.
   193	- **Check if a port is in use:**
   194	  ```bash
   195	  ss -ltnp | grep 15500
   196	  ```
   197	- **Kill a stuck viewer process:**
   198	  ```bash
   199	  fuser -k 15500/tcp
   200	  # or
   201	  kill $(ss -ltnp | grep ':15500 ' | grep -oP 'pid=\K\d+')
   202	  ```
   203	- **Custom port:**
   204	  ```bash
   205	  PROMPTFOO_PORT=16000 npm run view:promptfoo
   206	  ```
   207	- **No evals visible?** — run `npm run eval:promptfoo` first. The viewer reads
   208	  from the project-local DB at `promptfoo-evals/.promptfoo/`.
